generated_requirements.text: The system shall retrieve and display live event and activity data from both the Sync Matrix and Exercise Management Tool applications, refreshing the display automatically at intervals no greater than 30 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time data freshness is a core quality attribute; a maximum 30-second refresh cycle ensures coordinators act on current information during fast-moving exercises.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present a dual-region interface consisting of (1) a graphical timeline view and (2) a structured data table, both rendered within the same screen without requiring navigation between them.
generated_requirements.rationale: The dual-region layout is the defining interface paradigm described in the use case and must be explicitly specified as a functional requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The timeline view shall display all scheduled Events and Activities as horizontal bars positioned along a time axis, where each bar's position and width correspond to the activity's scheduled start time and duration respectively.
generated_requirements.rationale: Precise rendering rules for the timeline are necessary to ensure coordinators can accurately interpret scheduling relationships at a glance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall apply color-coded indicators to each activity in both the timeline view and the data table to reflect scheduling alignment status: green for on-schedule (within ±5 minutes of planned time), yellow for minor deviation (±6–15 minutes), and red for major deviation (more than ±15 minutes or cancelled/out-of-sequence).
generated_requirements.rationale: Color-coded scheduling alignment is explicitly called out in the use case as a key mechanism for coordinators to assess exercise execution health at a glance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to filter the display by one or more venues, showing only Events and Activities associated with the selected venue(s) in both the timeline and data table regions simultaneously.
generated_requirements.rationale: Venue-scoped filtering is required to support multi-venue exercises where coordinators need to focus on a specific operational area without distraction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users to filter the display to show only designated 'Key Events', hiding non-key activities from both the timeline and data table views while preserving the overall timeline scale.
generated_requirements.rationale: Key Event filtering reduces cognitive load during high-tempo exercise phases by surfacing only the most critical scheduled milestones.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control, granting Exercise Coordinators full read and filter access to all venues and events, while restricting Operations Staff to read and filter access for only the venues assigned to their role profile.
generated_requirements.rationale: The authorization model must differentiate between coordinator-level oversight and staff-level scoped access to prevent unauthorized visibility into unrelated exercise segments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users via the organization's existing identity provider using multi-factor authentication before granting access to any display data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Homeland security exercise data is sensitive; MFA and federated identity integration are baseline security controls required to protect access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide deep-link navigation controls within the data table that open the corresponding record directly in the source application (Sync Matrix or Exercise Management Tool) in a new browser tab, without requiring the user to re-authenticate.
generated_requirements.rationale: Deep-link navigation back to source applications is explicitly required by the use case to allow coordinators to initiate data modifications without leaving the integrated view.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect interruptions to live data feeds from either upstream source and display a clearly visible, timestamped warning banner indicating which feed is unavailable and the time of last successful data retrieval, within 60 seconds of feed failure.
generated_requirements.rationale: Failure-handling transparency is critical; coordinators must know immediately if displayed data is stale so they can take compensating actions during an exercise.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall remain operational in a read-only, last-known-good data mode for at least 10 minutes following a loss of connectivity to upstream data sources, continuing to display the most recently retrieved data.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fault tolerance ensures the display tool does not go completely dark during transient network or upstream outages, preserving situational awareness for coordinators.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an end-to-end display refresh latency — from data change in a source application to visible update in the user interface — of no more than 60 seconds under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: A defined end-to-end latency budget ensures the system meets the real-time performance quality attribute and sets a measurable acceptance criterion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support concurrent display sessions for at least 50 simultaneous authenticated users without degradation in refresh latency or interface responsiveness.
generated_requirements.rationale: Large exercises involve many coordinators and operations staff monitoring simultaneously; the system must scale to support realistic concurrent usage without performance degradation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall render the full dual-region interface, including timeline and data table with up to 200 activities, within 3 seconds of initial page load on a standard enterprise workstation over a LAN connection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Interface responsiveness directly impacts coordinator effectiveness; a 3-second load target ensures the tool is usable in time-pressured exercise environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall handle out-of-sequence activity states — where an activity is marked as started before its predecessor is complete — by flagging the affected activity with a distinct visual indicator (e.g., an exclamation icon) in both the timeline and data table views.
generated_requirements.rationale: Out-of-sequence execution is an explicit edge case that coordinators must be able to identify quickly to assess exercise fidelity and take corrective action.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate historical exercise reports that include a complete, time-ordered log of all Events and Activities, their planned versus actual start/end times, final color-coded status, and any state transitions (e.g., delayed, cancelled, out-of-sequence), exportable in PDF and CSV formats.
generated_requirements.rationale: Historical reporting and auditability are explicitly required quality attributes; structured export formats support post-exercise analysis and after-action reviews.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain an immutable audit log of all user authentication events, filter/customization actions, and report generation requests, retaining log entries for a minimum of 12 months.
generated_requirements.rationale: Auditability is a key quality attribute for homeland security tools; an immutable, time-stamped log supports accountability, forensic review, and compliance requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on any modern evergreen browser (Chrome, Edge, Firefox — latest two major versions) without requiring client-side plugin or software installation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Browser-based portability ensures the tool is accessible from diverse enterprise workstations and field laptops used during exercises without IT provisioning overhead.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose a documented integration API (REST or equivalent) for consuming data from the Sync Matrix and Exercise Management Tool, with versioned endpoints to prevent breaking changes from upstream application updates.
generated_requirements.rationale: A versioned integration contract decouples the display tool from upstream application release cycles, reducing maintenance risk and integration fragility over time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.5% during scheduled exercise windows, as measured over any rolling 30-day period, excluding pre-approved maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability during exercise execution is non-negotiable; downtime during a live homeland security exercise could compromise training objectives and coordinator decision-making.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall structure and label all exercise Events and Activities in conformance with the HSEEP doctrine taxonomy (e.g., distinguishing Master Scenario Events List (MSEL) injects from free-play activities), and shall display the MSEL inject identifier, responsible controller, and target capability for each activity in the data table.
generated_requirements.rationale: HSEEP doctrine mandates that exercises be designed and executed against a structured MSEL. A non-expert system may track generic 'events' without capturing HSEEP-specific metadata (inject ID, responsible controller, target capability). Without this, after-action review and the Improvement Plan (IP) cannot be properly tied back to specific MSEL injects, undermining the entire HSEEP cycle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the capture and display of Extent of Play (EOP) annotations against each activity, indicating whether the activity was executed fully, partially, or not at all, and shall include EOP status in all generated historical exercise reports.
generated_requirements.rationale: HSEEP requires that exercise documentation capture the Extent of Play agreed upon before the exercise and the actual extent achieved. Omitting EOP tracking means the After-Action Report (AAR) and Improvement Plan (IP) cannot accurately reflect what was and was not exercised, which is a compliance gap for federally funded exercises.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a 'Controller/Evaluator (C/E) Only' display mode, accessible exclusively to users with the Controller or Evaluator role, that reveals inject delivery details, expected player actions, and evaluation criteria that must not be visible to exercise participants (players) at any time.
generated_requirements.rationale: HSEEP exercises depend on strict information separation between controllers/evaluators and players to preserve exercise realism and validity. If inject details or expected actions are visible to players — even briefly — exercise integrity is compromised and results are invalidated. A generalist engineer would not anticipate this role-based content segregation requirement beyond simple read/filter access control.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a dedicated 'Hot Wash' data capture interface, accessible immediately upon exercise conclusion, that allows Controllers and Evaluators to record real-time observations, strengths, and areas for improvement against specific MSEL injects, and shall associate these observations with the corresponding activity records in the historical report.
generated_requirements.rationale: HSEEP doctrine requires a Hot Wash (immediate debrief) as a mandatory step in the exercise cycle. Capturing observations at the moment of exercise conclusion — while memory is fresh — is critical to producing a valid AAR/IP. Without an integrated capture mechanism, observations are lost or recorded in disconnected tools, degrading the quality of the Improvement Plan.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a configurable exercise clock that can be set to operate in real-time, accelerated (e.g., tabletop exercise time compression), or paused modes, and shall use this exercise clock — rather than wall-clock time — as the authoritative reference for all scheduling alignment calculations, color-coded status determinations, and timeline rendering.
generated_requirements.rationale: Many HSEEP exercise types (e.g., Tabletop Exercises, Functional Exercises) operate on a compressed or notional time scale that differs from real-world time. A system that uses only wall-clock time will produce incorrect scheduling alignment statuses and misleading color-coded indicators during these exercises, rendering the monitoring tool unreliable for the most common exercise formats.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall classify and handle all exercise data in accordance with the organization's data classification policy, and shall display a persistent classification banner (e.g., FOUO, CUI, or exercise-specific sensitivity label) on every screen and exported report, with the classification level configurable per exercise instance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Homeland security exercises frequently involve sensitive scenarios, real infrastructure data, and Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI). HSEEP and DHS policy require that exercise materials be appropriately marked and handled. Failure to display classification banners on screens and exports is a compliance violation and creates risk of inadvertent disclosure of sensitive exercise scenarios or vulnerability information.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an Exercise Director 'ENDEX' (End of Exercise) control that, when activated by an authorized Exercise Director role, immediately broadcasts a system-wide notification to all active display sessions indicating that the exercise has concluded, freezes the live data feed, and automatically initiates archival of the complete exercise dataset.
generated_requirements.rationale: ENDEX is a critical, time-sensitive command in exercise execution that must be communicated simultaneously to all participants and systems. Without a dedicated, role-protected ENDEX control, exercise conclusion may be ambiguous, data may continue to be modified post-exercise (corrupting the record), and the archival chain of custody required for AAR/IP development may be broken.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall produce exercise reports that are structured to directly support the HSEEP After-Action Report (AAR) and Improvement Plan (IP) format, including sections for observed strengths, areas for improvement, and corrective actions mapped to DHS Core Capabilities or jurisdiction-defined capability targets.
generated_requirements.rationale: HSEEP-compliant exercises are federally required to produce an AAR/IP within 60 days of exercise completion for grant-funded programs (e.g., BSIR, HSGP). If the system's historical reports do not align to the AAR/IP structure and Core Capabilities framework, coordinators must manually reformat all data, introducing delay and error risk, and potentially jeopardizing grant compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log and display a complete, timestamped record of all MSEL inject delivery events — including the controller who delivered the inject, the delivery method (e.g., phone, radio, written message), and the player or agency that received it — as part of the immutable audit trail and historical exercise record.
generated_requirements.rationale: HSEEP doctrine requires documentation of inject delivery as evidence that the exercise was conducted as designed. Without a delivery log, there is no way to distinguish between an activity that was not completed because the inject was never delivered versus one where the player failed to respond correctly — a distinction that is critical for valid AAR findings and defensible Improvement Plan recommendations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support an offline-capable, read-only display mode that can be pre-loaded with the full exercise dataset and operated without any network connectivity for a minimum of 4 hours, to accommodate exercise venues with degraded or absent network infrastructure, with automatic synchronization upon reconnection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Homeland security exercises are frequently conducted in austere or field environments (e.g., EOCs under simulated disaster conditions, remote venues) where network connectivity cannot be guaranteed. The existing 10-minute last-known-good mode is insufficient for full-day field exercises. HSEEP exercises must continue to be monitored and documented regardless of infrastructure degradation, mirroring the real-world conditions being exercised.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated realtors to search MLS listings by criteria including location, price range, property type, number of bedrooms/bathrooms, and square footage.
generated_requirements.rationale: Core field workflow: realtors must be able to query MLS data to identify and present relevant properties to clients.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display property detail pages including high-resolution images, interactive maps, and driving directions for each MLS listing.
generated_requirements.rationale: Realtors need rich visual and navigational information to evaluate properties and travel to showings while in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enable realtors to generate Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) reports by selecting comparable properties and automatically computing valuation metrics, exportable as a formatted PDF.
generated_requirements.rationale: CMA report generation is a primary value-add activity for realtors supporting property valuations and client presentations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow realtors to initiate calls to clients and send appointment requests directly from within the application, with clients able to respond and have responses reflected in the realtor's calendar.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integrated communication and scheduling reduces context-switching and supports end-to-end client relationship management from the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronize contact records and calendar data bidirectionally with the realtor's office systems, resolving conflicts using a last-write-wins strategy with a conflict log available for manual review.
generated_requirements.rationale: Bidirectional sync with a defined conflict resolution strategy ensures data integrity and keeps field and office data consistent.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a clearly defined offline mode in which realtors can access previously cached MLS listings, property images, maps, client contacts, and calendar entries without a network connection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Realtors frequently operate in areas with poor connectivity; offline access to cached data ensures continuous productivity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically detect network availability and, upon reconnection, synchronize all locally created or modified data (listings notes, appointments, CMA drafts) with office systems without requiring manual intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Seamless reconnection sync prevents data loss and removes the burden of manual upload from the realtor after returning to connectivity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate realtors using a username/password credential pair combined with multi-factor authentication (MFA), and shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) to restrict features and data to licensed, authorized realtors only.
generated_requirements.rationale: Access must be restricted to verified licensed realtors; MFA and RBAC satisfy both security and regulatory compliance requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a self-installing application package for each supported platform that completes installation without requiring IT assistance or formal user training, completing the full setup process in under 5 minutes.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly requires self-installation and no formal training, making a guided, zero-touch setup process a functional necessity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return MLS search results within 3 seconds for queries executed under normal connected network conditions (≥ 4G/LTE).
generated_requirements.rationale: Slow search responses degrade the realtor's ability to serve clients in real time; a 3-second threshold aligns with mobile UX best practices.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall encrypt all data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher and all data at rest on the device using AES-256 encryption, including cached listings, client contacts, and authentication tokens.
generated_requirements.rationale: Sensitive client and property data stored on mobile devices and transmitted over public networks must be protected against interception and unauthorized access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically lock and require re-authentication after 5 minutes of user inactivity, and shall provide a remote wipe capability for lost or stolen devices.
generated_requirements.rationale: Mobile devices are easily lost or stolen; automatic locking and remote wipe protect sensitive real estate and client data from unauthorized access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a task-completion rate of ≥ 90% for first-time users performing core workflows (MLS search, CMA generation, appointment scheduling) without any external guidance or training materials.
generated_requirements.rationale: The application is explicitly designed to require no formal training; a measurable first-use task-completion rate validates that the UI is sufficiently intuitive.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain an application uptime of 99.9% (measured monthly), excluding scheduled maintenance windows communicated at least 24 hours in advance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Realtors depend on the system during client meetings and property showings; high availability is critical to uninterrupted field productivity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall scale horizontally to support a minimum of 10,000 concurrent authenticated users without degradation in response time or data consistency.
generated_requirements.rationale: The platform must support thousands of simultaneous users as stated in the use case; horizontal scalability ensures performance is maintained under peak load.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable and fully functional on iOS (version 15 and above), Android (version 11 and above), and Windows tablet platforms, with a consistent feature set and UI across all supported platforms.
generated_requirements.rationale: Realtors use diverse smart devices; cross-platform support with a consistent experience ensures broad adoption without platform-specific limitations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all data entered during offline operation against business rules (e.g., required fields, date format, numeric ranges) at the point of entry and re-validate against server-side rules upon synchronization, rejecting and flagging any records that fail validation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dual-layer validation prevents corrupt or invalid data from propagating into office systems, preserving data integrity across connected and disconnected modes.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall follow a modular architecture with documented APIs for each major component (MLS integration, CMA engine, sync service, authentication), enabling individual modules to be updated or replaced without impacting other components.
generated_requirements.rationale: A modular, well-documented architecture reduces the cost and risk of future enhancements, third-party MLS integrations, and platform updates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all authentication attempts (successful and failed), data synchronization events, and remote wipe actions to a tamper-evident audit log retained for a minimum of 12 months.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit logs support forensic investigation of security incidents, demonstrate regulatory compliance, and provide accountability for sensitive data access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from an unexpected crash or forced closure and restore the user's last active session state (including unsaved search filters and open property records) within 10 seconds of relaunch.
generated_requirements.rationale: Crash recovery with session restoration minimizes disruption during client-facing interactions and prevents loss of in-progress work in the field.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display MLS listing data in strict compliance with the applicable MLS's IDX (Internet Data Exchange) and VOW (Virtual Office Website) rules, including mandatory display of the listing broker's name, MLS logo, listing attribution, and 'last updated' timestamp on every property record, and shall suppress or expire listings within the timeframe required by each MLS board (typically within 15 minutes of status change).
generated_requirements.rationale: Every MLS board enforces IDX/VOW display rules as a condition of data feed access. Failure to comply — such as omitting listing broker attribution or displaying stale sold/expired listings — can result in immediate termination of the MLS data feed, fines, and loss of the realtor's MLS membership. A generalist engineer would not know these rules exist, let alone their specifics.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict MLS listing data access and display exclusively to licensed, active realtors with a valid MLS membership in good standing, and shall integrate with the MLS's participant/subscriber roster (or NRDS — National REALTORS® Database System) to automatically suspend access for any realtor whose license or MLS membership lapses, is suspended, or is revoked, within 24 hours of the status change.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS participation agreements legally require that data access be limited to active, licensed members. Allowing a lapsed or suspended licensee to continue accessing MLS data exposes the brokerage and technology provider to MLS policy violations and potential state real estate commission sanctions. Automated roster sync is the only scalable enforcement mechanism.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ensure that CMA reports generated and exported as PDFs include all disclosures required by applicable state real estate commission regulations, including a prominent disclaimer stating that the CMA is not a certified appraisal, the date of report generation, the identity and license number of the generating realtor, and the brokerage name and license number.
generated_requirements.rationale: Most state real estate commissions mandate specific disclosures on CMAs to prevent consumers from mistaking them for licensed appraisals (which would constitute the unauthorized practice of appraisal). Missing these disclosures can result in disciplinary action against the realtor and brokerage. A generalist engineer would treat CMA generation as a pure data/formatting problem and miss the legal disclosure layer entirely.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with the National Association of REALTORS® (NAR) MLS Policy Statement 7.58 and applicable state regulations by ensuring that any consumer-facing or client-shared property data (e.g., CMA reports, shared listing links) includes the required fair housing statement and equal opportunity housing logo, and that the application itself displays the Equal Housing Opportunity logo in a persistent, visible location.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Fair Housing Act and NAR Code of Ethics require that all real estate marketing materials and platforms display the Equal Housing Opportunity logo and statement. Omission constitutes a Fair Housing violation, which carries federal penalties and NAR disciplinary consequences. This is a domain-specific legal obligation invisible to a generalist engineer.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall treat all client personal data (names, contact details, financial qualifications, communication history) in accordance with applicable privacy regulations including CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) and any state-level equivalents, providing mechanisms for realtors to fulfill client data deletion and data export requests within the legally mandated response windows (e.g., 45 days under CCPA), and shall not share or sell client data to third parties without explicit, documented client consent.
generated_requirements.rationale: Realtors collect sensitive consumer financial and personal data (pre-qualification amounts, income disclosures, family details) that is subject to state privacy laws. A real estate application operating at scale across multiple states must have explicit data subject rights workflows. The existing requirements address encryption and audit logging but do not address consumer privacy rights or data handling obligations — a critical compliance gap.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall flag and visually distinguish MLS listings that have undergone a status change (e.g., Active to Pending, Pending to Sold, Price Reduced, Back on Market) since the realtor last viewed them, and shall prevent realtors from presenting or sharing listing data that has been marked as Sold, Expired, or Withdrawn for more than 24 hours as if it were currently active inventory.
generated_requirements.rationale: Presenting a sold or expired listing to a client as active inventory — even unintentionally due to stale cached data — constitutes misrepresentation under NAR's Code of Ethics (Article 1) and can expose the realtor to client complaints and disciplinary proceedings. The existing offline caching requirement does not address the risk of stale listing status; this requirement closes that gap with explicit staleness controls.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record and retain a complete, timestamped audit trail of all MLS data queries made by each realtor (including search parameters, listing records accessed, and client-shared reports), stored in a tamper-evident log retained for a minimum of 3 years, to support MLS compliance audits and brokerage supervision obligations under state real estate law.
generated_requirements.rationale: MLS boards and state real estate commissions have the authority to audit member data usage to detect unauthorized scraping, data resale, or discriminatory search patterns (e.g., steering). Brokerages are also required by state law to supervise agent activity. A 12-month authentication log (already required) is insufficient; MLS data access activity requires a longer retention window aligned with statute of limitations for real estate violations, typically 3–5 years.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with RESO (Real Estate Standards Organization) Data Dictionary standards for all MLS data fields, ensuring that property attributes (e.g., ListingStatus, PropertyType, LivingArea, ListPrice) use RESO-standardized field names and enumerated values, to guarantee interoperability across multiple MLS boards and prevent data mapping errors when operating in multi-MLS environments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Realtors operating near MLS jurisdiction boundaries (e.g., metro areas spanning multiple counties or states) must pull data from multiple MLS boards simultaneously. Without RESO Data Dictionary compliance, field names and value enumerations differ between boards (e.g., 'SqFt' vs. 'LivingArea'), causing silent data mapping errors that corrupt search results and CMA calculations. RESO compliance is now a mandatory certification requirement for MLS technology vendors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a Do Not Contact (DNC) compliance check prior to initiating any outbound call or SMS to a client, cross-referencing the client's phone number against the National Do Not Call Registry and any brokerage-maintained internal suppression lists, and shall block the communication and log the attempted contact if a match is found.
generated_requirements.rationale: The FTC's Telephone Consumer Protection Act (TCPA) and Do Not Call regulations impose significant per-violation fines (up to $43,792 per call) on real estate professionals who contact numbers on the DNC registry. Since the system enables realtors to initiate calls directly from the application, it must enforce DNC compliance at the point of initiation. A generalist engineer building a calling feature would not consider this regulatory obligation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the display and filtering of MLS listing data by school district boundaries, flood zone designation (FEMA flood zone classification), and HOA (Homeowners Association) presence and estimated dues, sourcing this data from authoritative third-party providers and clearly attributing the data source and effective date on all property detail pages.
generated_requirements.rationale: School district, flood zone, and HOA information are among the top decision-driving factors for homebuyers and are material facts that realtors have a duty to disclose under most state real estate laws. Flood zone designation in particular affects insurability and mortgage eligibility (FEMA NFIP requirements). Omitting these data points from a professional realtor tool creates liability exposure and reduces the system's utility for client-facing presentations. These fields are not captured in the generic search criteria listed in the existing requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to create, edit, and delete Programs of Study, including defining associated courses, credit hours, and quarter sequences.
generated_requirements.rationale: Core lifecycle management of Programs of Study is a primary administrative function described in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to create and manage student cohorts, including adding or removing students, assigning cohorts to a Program of Study, and tracking each cohort's current quarter progression.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cohort organization and lifecycle management is a central capability of the platform for administrative planning.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enable Program Administrators and Nursing Staff Members to schedule both classroom and clinical course sections across multiple quarters, including specifying meeting times, locations, instructors, and enrollment capacities.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-quarter scheduling of classroom and clinical sections is a primary scheduling function identified in the use case description.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall manage clinical site assignments by allowing Program Administrators to associate clinical course sections with approved clinical sites, assign students to specific sites, and enforce site-level capacity constraints.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clinical site assignment management is explicitly called out as a key capability requiring capacity enforcement and student-level tracking.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Program Administrators to create, modify, and dissolve lab sections, assigning students and instructors to each section and linking lab sections to their parent course.
generated_requirements.rationale: Lab section management is identified as a distinct scheduling concern requiring full CRUD support and linkage to parent courses.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall track individual student progress through their assigned Program of Study, recording completed courses, current enrollments, and outstanding requirements per quarter.
generated_requirements.rationale: Individual student progress tracking is explicitly listed as a platform capability to support administrative planning.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically send email notifications to affected students, instructors, and relevant staff within 5 minutes of any change to a schedule, clinical section assignment, or cohort arrangement.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automated email notification upon changes is a core alerting requirement; the 5-minute threshold ensures timely communication as implied by the reliability quality attribute.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate printable reports for cohorts, individual students, clinical sites, and lab sections; each report type shall include relevant scheduling, enrollment, and progress data, and shall be exportable in at least PDF and CSV formats.
generated_requirements.rationale: Report generation across all four entity types in printable and exportable formats is explicitly required to support administrative planning.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) distinguishing at minimum two roles — Program Administrator and Nursing Staff Member — where Program Administrators have full create/read/update/delete privileges and Nursing Staff Members have read and limited update privileges as defined by the administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Role-based access control with clearly delineated privilege boundaries is a key security requirement protecting sensitive student and clinical site data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate bidirectionally with DePaul University's CampusConnect system to synchronize student enrollment records, course catalog data, and instructor assignments, with synchronization occurring at least once every 24 hours and on-demand when triggered by an administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: CampusConnect integration is explicitly identified as a required touchpoint; bidirectional sync with defined frequency ensures data consistency across university systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with DePaul University faculty and student servers to retrieve and update faculty profiles, student records, and authentication credentials, and shall handle integration failures gracefully by queuing failed transactions and retrying automatically without data loss.
generated_requirements.rationale: Faculty and student server integration is required for data exchange; failure-handling with retry queuing addresses the reliability quality attribute for integration touchpoints.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect and surface scheduling conflicts (e.g., instructor double-booking, room overlap, student assigned to concurrent sections) in real time during schedule creation or modification, preventing the save of a conflicting record until the conflict is resolved or explicitly overridden by a Program Administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Conflict detection is a critical scheduling integrity requirement implied by the multi-quarter, multi-section scheduling lifecycle described in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users via DePaul University's existing Single Sign-On (SSO) mechanism and shall not store plaintext credentials locally.
generated_requirements.rationale: SSO integration with the university's identity provider ensures consistent credential management and eliminates local plaintext credential risks, aligning with the security quality attribute.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall have an availability of at least 99.5% during the university's defined academic operational hours (Monday–Friday, 7:00 AM–10:00 PM CT), with planned maintenance windows scheduled outside these hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability during operational hours is essential for day-to-day nursing program administration as described in the use case, supporting the reliability quality attribute.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall load any page or complete any user-initiated action (e.g., saving a schedule, generating a report) within 3 seconds under normal load conditions (up to 100 concurrent users).
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsive performance under expected concurrent load is necessary for a usable day-to-day administrative tool used by program staff.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards and shall provide a consistent, task-oriented layout that allows a trained Program Administrator to complete common scheduling tasks (e.g., creating a cohort, assigning a clinical site) within 5 minutes without consulting documentation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ease of use is explicitly called out as a quality attribute; WCAG compliance and task-completion benchmarks operationalize the usability requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete, tamper-evident audit log of all create, update, and delete operations on cohorts, schedules, clinical site assignments, and student records, capturing the acting user, timestamp, and before/after values, and retaining logs for a minimum of 3 years.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit logging protects sensitive student and clinical data, supports accountability, and satisfies likely FERPA-related compliance obligations for a university nursing program.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall follow a modular architecture with documented APIs for each integration point (CampusConnect, faculty server, student server), such that any single integration module can be updated or replaced without requiring changes to core scheduling or cohort management logic.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular architecture with documented APIs reduces the cost of future integration changes and supports long-term maintainability of the platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on DePaul University's existing server infrastructure (Linux-based, supporting standard web application stacks) without requiring proprietary hardware or operating system licenses beyond those already in use by the university.
generated_requirements.rationale: Portability to the university's existing infrastructure avoids additional procurement costs and ensures the platform can be hosted and maintained by existing IT staff.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall deliver all automated email notifications with an accuracy rate of at least 99% (i.e., no missed or incorrectly addressed notifications), and shall log each notification attempt including delivery status, recipient, and triggering event for auditing purposes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Notification accuracy is explicitly listed as a key quality attribute; a measurable accuracy threshold and delivery logging ensure the alerting subsystem is dependable and auditable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall track and enforce clinical site affiliation agreement (contract) expiration dates for each approved clinical site, automatically alerting Program Administrators at least 90 days before expiration and preventing the assignment of students to any clinical site whose affiliation agreement has expired or is not on file.
generated_requirements.rationale: ACEN and CCNE accreditation standards require that all clinical sites used by nursing programs have current, executed affiliation agreements on file. Assigning students to a site without a valid agreement exposes the university to accreditation citations, liability, and potential loss of clinical placement. A generalist engineer would not know this contractual compliance layer exists.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a compliance record for each student that tracks the currency and expiration of required clinical clearance items — including but not limited to immunization records, CPR/BLS certification, background check clearance, drug screening results, and clinical site-specific health requirements — and shall prevent assignment of a non-compliant student to a clinical section, surfacing a clear compliance gap report to the Program Administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clinical sites universally require students to meet health and safety clearance requirements before setting foot on site. ACEN Standard 4 and CCNE Standard III both address student safety and clinical readiness. Placing a student who lacks current immunizations or a cleared background check creates legal liability for the university and the clinical partner, and can result in immediate removal of the student and loss of the clinical site relationship.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record and enforce the maximum allowable student-to-faculty ratio for each clinical course section, as defined per clinical site agreement and applicable Illinois nursing education regulations (e.g., Illinois Administrative Code Title 77, Part 395), and shall prevent saving a clinical section assignment that would cause the ratio to be exceeded.
generated_requirements.rationale: Illinois nursing program regulations and most clinical site contracts specify maximum student-to-clinical-instructor ratios (commonly 1:8 or 1:10). Exceeding these ratios is a direct accreditation and regulatory violation. A generalist engineer would model capacity as a simple integer cap without understanding that ratio enforcement is a distinct, legally mandated constraint tied to the instructor assignment, not just the room or site.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the recording and reporting of NCLEX first-time pass rate data by cohort and graduation year, and shall generate an accreditation-ready summary report mapping cohort completion rates, attrition rates, and on-time graduation rates to the program's defined benchmarks, exportable in formats suitable for ACEN or CCNE self-study submission.
generated_requirements.rationale: ACEN Standard 6 and CCNE Standard IV require programs to continuously track and report outcome metrics including NCLEX pass rates, program completion rates, and graduation rates against established benchmarks. These reports are central to accreditation self-studies and annual reports. Without structured data capture and reporting for these outcomes, the program must manually compile data from disparate sources, introducing error and audit risk.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce and document that each clinical course section is supervised by a faculty member who holds a current, unencumbered Registered Nurse (RN) license in the state of Illinois, and shall store faculty licensure details (license number, expiration date, issuing state) with automated alerts to Program Administrators at least 60 days before any faculty clinical supervisor's license expires.
generated_requirements.rationale: Illinois Administrative Code Title 77, Part 395 requires that clinical instruction in approved nursing programs be provided only by faculty holding a current, unencumbered RN license. Assigning an instructor with a lapsed or encumbered license to a clinical section is a regulatory violation that can trigger program probation or closure. A generalist engineer would treat faculty credentials as a profile field rather than a compliance-enforced constraint.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a historical, immutable record of each student's clinical hour log per course section, capturing the date, clinical site, supervising instructor, and hours completed, and shall aggregate these hours to verify that each student meets the minimum direct patient care clinical hours required by the program curriculum and Illinois nursing education regulations before the student is marked eligible for program completion.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accreditation bodies and state boards of nursing require documented evidence that each graduate has completed a minimum number of supervised clinical hours. This is distinct from course enrollment or grade recording — it is an hour-level audit trail. Without this, the program cannot demonstrate compliance during accreditation site visits or respond to a state board audit of a graduate's licensure application.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall restrict access to individually identifiable student health and compliance records (immunization status, drug screening results, background check outcomes) to only those roles explicitly authorized by the Program Administrator, and shall treat such records as protected health information subject to FERPA and applicable HIPAA-adjacent institutional privacy policies, logging every access event to these records in the audit trail.
generated_requirements.rationale: Student health clearance records collected for clinical placement contain sensitive health information. While FERPA governs student educational records broadly, health records held by a university's health or nursing program occupy a complex FERPA/HIPAA boundary. Unauthorized disclosure of immunization status, drug test results, or background check outcomes creates significant legal exposure. A generalist engineer would apply standard RBAC without recognizing the heightened sensitivity and access-logging obligations specific to this data category.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the configuration and tracking of program-defined 'progression policies' — including minimum GPA thresholds, required course prerequisites, and mandatory remediation milestones — and shall automatically flag any student whose record indicates a progression policy violation, notifying the Program Administrator and generating a documented advisement record that can be attached to the student's academic file.
generated_requirements.rationale: Nursing programs operate under strict progression policies required by accreditation standards (ACEN Standard 4, CCNE Standard III) to ensure patient safety competency. Students who fail to meet GPA or prerequisite thresholds must not advance to clinical courses. Without automated progression gating and documented advisement records, programs risk accreditation findings related to inadequate academic standards enforcement and lack of documented student support processes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a structured mechanism for recording, tracking, and resolving clinical incident reports — including student injuries, patient safety events, and site-reported behavioral concerns occurring during clinical rotations — linking each incident to the relevant student, clinical site, course section, and supervising instructor, and retaining incident records for a minimum of 7 years.
generated_requirements.rationale: Clinical incidents during student rotations carry significant liability and accreditation implications. ACEN and CCNE standards require programs to have documented processes for managing student safety events. Clinical site partners frequently require incident documentation as a condition of the affiliation agreement. A 7-year retention period aligns with common institutional risk management and potential statute of limitations considerations. A generalist engineer would have no basis for knowing this workflow needs to exist within the scheduling platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) such that internal cardmember services representatives may only view and modify dispute cases associated with cardholders within their authorized cardholder base; external merchant services representatives may only view and modify dispute cases associated with merchants within their authorized merchant portfolio; and system administrators may view all cases but must perform privileged actions through a separately authenticated admin session.
generated_requirements.rationale: Directly addresses the role-based data-visibility boundary requirement across all three user personas described in the use case, and supports least-privilege security principles.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow internal cardmember services representatives and system administrators to initiate a new credit card dispute case by providing at minimum: the disputed transaction identifier, cardholder account number, dispute reason code, dispute amount, and transaction date. The system shall validate all mandatory fields before persisting the case and shall reject case creation if the disputed transaction cannot be located in the cardmember database.
generated_requirements.rationale: Captures the case-creation entry point of the dispute lifecycle and enforces data integrity by requiring back-end transaction validation before a case is opened.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users (internal cardmember services representatives and system administrators) to create a ticket retrieval request against an open dispute case. The request shall be transmitted to the merchant database integration layer within 60 seconds of submission, and the system shall record the request timestamp, requesting user identity, and target merchant identifier in the case audit trail.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defines the ticket retrieval request workflow, specifies the responsible roles, sets a transmission SLA, and mandates audit-trail capture for this lifecycle action.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized users (internal cardmember services representatives and system administrators) to issue a chargeback notification against an open dispute case. Before issuing the notification, the system shall automatically verify that all applicable regulatory deadlines (Regulation E: 45-day provisional credit and 90-day final resolution; Regulation Z: 60-day billing-error notice; Merchant Operating Regulations chargeback time limits) have not been exceeded, and shall prevent issuance and display a compliance warning if any deadline is breached.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encodes the regulatory deadline enforcement for Reg E, Reg Z, and Merchant Operating Regulations into the chargeback notification workflow, ensuring compliance is a system-enforced control rather than a manual check.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the following dispute case lifecycle states and enforce valid state transitions: [New → Under Review → Pending Merchant Response → Chargeback Issued → Resolved | Closed]. Unauthorized or out-of-sequence state transitions shall be rejected with an explanatory error message. Each state transition shall be timestamped and attributed to the initiating user or automated process in the audit trail.
generated_requirements.rationale: Formalizes the end-to-end dispute lifecycle state machine, preventing invalid transitions and ensuring every state change is fully auditable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a case search interface accessible to all authenticated users, returning only cases within the user's authorized scope (per R001). Search criteria shall include at minimum: case ID, cardholder account number, merchant ID, dispute status, dispute reason code, and date range. Search results shall be returned within 3 seconds for result sets up to 500 records under normal load.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the search capability described in the use case while embedding the data-visibility boundary and a measurable performance expectation for the search operation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete, immutable audit trail for every dispute case, recording all user-initiated and system-generated events including: case creation, field updates (capturing old and new values), state transitions, ticket retrieval requests, chargeback notifications, integration call outcomes, letter generation events, and case closure. Audit records shall include the event timestamp (UTC), user identity or system process name, and the IP address of the originating request. Audit records shall not be modifiable or deletable by any user role, including system administrators.
generated_requirements.rationale: Treats audit-trail completeness as a first-class functional requirement, satisfying regulatory auditability obligations under Reg E, Reg Z, and Merchant Operating Regulations, and supporting forensic investigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the cardmember database to retrieve cardholder account details (name, account status, billing address, transaction history) at case creation and on demand. If the cardmember database is unavailable, the system shall display a service-unavailability message, prevent case creation, and log the integration failure with a timestamp and error code. The system shall retry the connection up to 3 times with exponential back-off before declaring the service unavailable.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defines the integration contract with the cardmember database, including the data exchanged, failure-handling behaviour, and retry policy to ensure transactional consistency.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the merchant database to retrieve merchant account details (merchant name, category code, contact information, chargeback history) and to transmit ticket retrieval requests and chargeback notifications. All outbound messages shall conform to the agreed merchant database API schema version. Failed transmissions shall be queued for retry (up to 3 attempts) and flagged in the case audit trail; persistent failures shall trigger an alert to the system administrator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Specifies the integration contract with the merchant database, covering data retrieval, outbound message transmission, schema conformance, retry logic, and escalation on persistent failure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the billing system to apply provisional credits, reverse credits, and post final financial adjustments as required by dispute outcomes. Each financial transaction submitted to the billing system shall include the case ID, adjustment amount, adjustment type, and authorizing user identity. The system shall confirm successful posting before updating the dispute case status, and shall roll back the case status update if the billing system returns an error.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defines the billing system integration contract with transactional consistency requirements, ensuring financial adjustments and case status remain synchronized and preventing partial-update data integrity failures.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the letter printing utility to generate and dispatch regulatory correspondence (e.g., acknowledgment letters, provisional credit notices, chargeback notifications to merchants) as required by Regulation E, Regulation Z, and Merchant Operating Regulations. Letter generation requests shall include the case ID, recipient details, letter template identifier, and generation timestamp. The system shall record the letter type, generation timestamp, and delivery status in the case audit trail.
generated_requirements.rationale: Captures the letter printing utility integration contract, ensuring regulatory correspondence is generated with the correct data, and that all correspondence events are auditable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically calculate and display the remaining time to each applicable regulatory deadline (Reg E provisional credit deadline, Reg E final resolution deadline, Reg Z billing-error response deadline, Merchant Operating Regulations chargeback deadline) on the case detail view, and shall generate automated in-application alerts to the assigned representative and their supervisor when a deadline is within 5 business days of expiry.
generated_requirements.rationale: Provides proactive regulatory deadline management, reducing the risk of compliance violations by surfacing time-sensitive obligations to responsible users before they are breached.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the Disputes application and end-user browsers, and between the application and all back-end integration systems (cardmember DB, merchant DB, billing system, letter printing utility), shall be encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher. Sensitive cardholder data (PAN, account number) shall be masked in all UI displays, showing only the last four digits, and shall be encrypted at rest using AES-256.
generated_requirements.rationale: Protects sensitive financial and cardholder data in transit and at rest, satisfying PCI-DSS and financial regulatory security obligations applicable to credit card dispute systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users via the organization's centralized identity provider using multi-factor authentication (MFA) before granting access to any dispute case data or functionality. User sessions shall expire after 15 minutes of inactivity, requiring re-authentication. Failed login attempts shall be locked out after 5 consecutive failures, and all authentication events (success, failure, lockout) shall be logged in the audit trail.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enforces strong authentication controls appropriate for a financial case management system handling sensitive cardholder and merchant data, and ensures authentication events are auditable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall process and render any individual dispute case detail page within 2 seconds at the 95th percentile under a concurrent load of up to 200 simultaneous authenticated users. Batch operations (e.g., bulk status updates initiated by system administrators) shall complete within 30 seconds for batches of up to 1,000 cases.
generated_requirements.rationale: Sets measurable performance benchmarks for the primary user-facing operation and for administrative batch processing, ensuring the system remains responsive under expected operational load.

generated_requirements.text: The Disputes application shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.5% measured on a monthly basis during defined business hours (06:00–22:00 local time, Monday–Saturday). Planned maintenance windows shall be scheduled outside business hours, and the system shall display a maintenance notification at least 24 hours in advance. Unplanned outages shall trigger automated alerting to the operations team within 2 minutes of detection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defines the availability SLA required for a business-critical financial case management system, with explicit maintenance and alerting obligations to minimize service disruption.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be recoverable to a consistent state within a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of 4 hours and a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of 1 hour in the event of a catastrophic failure. Database backups shall be performed at minimum every 60 minutes and stored in a geographically separate location. Backup restoration procedures shall be tested at least quarterly.
generated_requirements.rationale: Establishes disaster recovery targets appropriate for a regulated financial system where data loss or extended downtime could result in regulatory violations and financial harm to cardholders.

generated_requirements.text: All back-end integration interfaces (cardmember DB, merchant DB, billing system, letter printing utility) shall be implemented using versioned, documented APIs (RESTful or message-queue based). Integration configurations (endpoints, credentials, retry policies, timeout thresholds) shall be externalized into environment-specific configuration files and shall not require application code changes to update. New integration versions shall be deployable without downtime using a blue-green or canary deployment strategy.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ensures integration flexibility and reduces the cost of change when back-end systems evolve, supporting long-term maintainability of the application and minimizing deployment risk.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on any major cloud provider (AWS, Azure, or GCP) or on-premises infrastructure using containerized deployment (Docker/Kubernetes) without modification to application source code. Environment-specific settings shall be injected via environment variables or a secrets management service. The application shall support horizontal scaling by adding container instances without architectural changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ensures the application is not locked to a single infrastructure provider, supporting organizational flexibility and enabling scaling to meet demand growth without re-engineering.

generated_requirements.text: The user interface shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards and shall provide contextual inline guidance (field-level help text, regulatory deadline explanations, and error messages written in plain language) to enable internal and external representatives with varying levels of system expertise to complete dispute management tasks without requiring escalation to a supervisor for routine operations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ensures the application is accessible to users with disabilities and is sufficiently self-explanatory for the diverse user population (internal reps, external merchant reps) to operate effectively, reducing training costs and error rates.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically apply a provisional credit to the cardholder's account within 5 business days of receiving a written notice of a billing error (or within 10 business days if no provisional credit is to be issued and the investigation is completed), and within 1 business day for alleged unauthorized electronic fund transfer disputes, in accordance with Regulation E (12 CFR §1005.11) and Regulation Z (12 CFR §1026.13). The system shall record the provisional credit application date, amount, and associated case ID in the audit trail and shall trigger the appropriate regulatory correspondence via the letter printing utility.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reg E mandates provisional credit within 10 business days (or 5 for certain POS disputes) and Reg Z requires provisional credit within 2 complete billing cycles but no later than 90 days. Failure to apply provisional credit within the mandated window is a direct regulatory violation exposing the institution to liability. The existing requirements reference deadlines for chargeback issuance but do not explicitly enforce the provisional credit application trigger and timing, which is a distinct and critical compliance obligation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the full second-presentment (re-presentment) and pre-arbitration / arbitration lifecycle stages as extensions of the dispute case state machine, including the states: [Chargeback Issued → Second Presentment Received → Pre-Arbitration Filed → Arbitration → Resolved | Closed]. The system shall record the receipt date of any second presentment, calculate and display the pre-arbitration filing deadline per applicable card network rules, and prevent pre-arbitration filing after the network deadline has elapsed. All arbitration case reference numbers assigned by the card network shall be stored against the dispute case record.
generated_requirements.rationale: The existing state machine terminates at 'Chargeback Issued → Resolved | Closed', which omits the merchant's right to re-present (second presentment) and the subsequent pre-arbitration and arbitration stages defined in all major card network Operating Regulations. Missing these stages means the system cannot manage the full dispute lifecycle, will fail to enforce pre-arbitration deadlines (typically 30–45 days from second presentment depending on network), and will expose the institution to automatic loss of arbitration cases due to missed filing windows — a significant financial and compliance risk.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect and flag potential duplicate dispute cases at the time of case creation by checking whether an open or recently closed (within 12 months) dispute case already exists for the same combination of cardholder account number, disputed transaction identifier, and dispute reason code. When a potential duplicate is detected, the system shall present the existing case(s) to the initiating user for review, require explicit confirmation or a documented justification before allowing the new case to proceed, and record the duplicate-detection event and user decision in the audit trail.
generated_requirements.rationale: Duplicate dispute filings are a significant operational and compliance risk: they can result in double provisional credits being applied to a cardholder account (financial loss), multiple chargebacks being transmitted to the same merchant for the same transaction (a violation of card network rules that can result in network fines and merchant relationship damage), and inflated dispute volumes that distort regulatory reporting. Card network rules explicitly prohibit filing more than one chargeback per transaction per dispute reason. A generalist engineer would not typically model duplicate detection as a domain-specific constraint.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce the Regulation E error resolution notice requirement by automatically generating and dispatching (via the letter printing utility) a written acknowledgment of the dispute to the cardholder within 5 business days of receiving the dispute, and a final resolution notice within 3 business days of completing the investigation, in accordance with 12 CFR §1005.11(c) and (d). For Regulation Z billing-error disputes, the system shall generate an acknowledgment within 30 days of receipt and a resolution notice within 2 complete billing cycles (not to exceed 90 days). The system shall track and record the dispatch date of each required notice and shall generate an escalation alert if any notice has not been confirmed as dispatched within the required window.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reg E §1005.11(c) and Reg Z §1026.13(c) impose specific written-notice obligations with hard deadlines that are distinct from the provisional credit and final resolution deadlines. Failure to send timely acknowledgment or resolution notices is an independent regulatory violation that can trigger CFPB examination findings, consumer complaints, and civil liability. The existing requirements reference letter generation generically but do not model the specific notice types, their triggering events, or their individual compliance deadlines — a critical gap from a compliance officer's perspective.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall capture and store the cardholder's dispute intake method (e.g., written correspondence, oral telephone claim, online portal submission) and, for oral Regulation E claims, shall enforce a workflow requiring the cardholder services representative to send a written confirmation to the cardholder within 10 business days, as required by 12 CFR §1005.11(b)(2). The intake method and confirmation dispatch date shall be recorded in the case record and audit trail. The system shall prevent the dispute investigation clock from starting until the intake method is recorded.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reg E §1005.11(b) distinguishes between written and oral error notices and imposes an additional written-confirmation obligation for oral claims. The investigation timeline and provisional credit deadlines are calculated from the date the institution receives the written notice (or written confirmation of an oral notice). Failing to capture the intake method means the system cannot correctly anchor the regulatory clock, potentially causing the institution to either start the clock too early (disadvantaging the cardholder) or too late (violating the regulation). This is a nuanced compliance detail that a generalist engineer would almost certainly overlook.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall produce a regulatory reporting data extract, at minimum on a monthly basis and on demand, that includes: total dispute volume by reason code and card network, provisional credit amounts applied and reversed, average and maximum time-to-resolution, cases where any Regulation E, Regulation Z, or card network deadline was breached (with breach duration), and cases escalated to pre-arbitration or arbitration. The extract shall be exportable in a structured format (CSV and JSON) and shall be access-restricted to system administrators and compliance officers. All extract generation events shall be logged in the audit trail.
generated_requirements.rationale: Financial institutions are subject to regulatory examination by the CFPB, OCC, Federal Reserve, and card networks, all of which require evidence of dispute management compliance through metrics and case-level data. Card networks (Visa, Mastercard) also impose chargeback ratio monitoring programs (e.g., Visa's Dispute Monitoring Program, Mastercard's Excessive Chargeback Program) with financial penalties for institutions exceeding defined thresholds. Without a structured compliance reporting capability, the institution cannot demonstrate regulatory adherence, monitor its own network compliance standing, or respond to examiner requests — a significant supervisory risk.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a data retention policy whereby all dispute case records, associated documents, audit trail entries, and regulatory correspondence are retained for a minimum of 5 years from the date of case closure, in compliance with applicable federal record-keeping requirements (12 CFR §1005.13 for Reg E, 12 CFR §1026.25 for Reg Z) and card network Operating Regulations. Retained records shall remain queryable by authorized compliance and audit users. The system shall prevent deletion or archival of any case record before the minimum retention period has elapsed, and shall generate an automated alert to the compliance officer 30 days before any record is eligible for purge.
generated_requirements.rationale: Reg E §1005.13 and Reg Z §1026.25 both mandate a minimum 2-year record retention period, but card network Operating Regulations (Visa, Mastercard) and general banking examination practice require 5 years for dispute and chargeback records. The existing requirements specify that audit records are immutable but do not define a retention period or a retention enforcement mechanism. Without explicit retention enforcement, records could be purged prematurely during routine database maintenance, destroying evidence needed for regulatory examinations, litigation holds, or network dispute arbitration — a serious legal and compliance exposure.
