generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow collision estimators to add one or more recycled parts retrieved from the ChoiceParts feed to an active collision repair estimate.
generated_requirements.rationale: Applying recycled parts to estimates is the primary functional responsibility of the collision estimator role.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow adjusters to assign a preferred repair facility to an insurance claim prior to estimate review.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adjusters are responsible for directing claims to appropriate repair facilities as part of their workflow.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow adjusters to review completed collision repair estimates, including the list of parts used, their classification (recycled vs. new), and associated costs.
generated_requirements.rationale: Estimate review is a core adjuster responsibility; visibility into part classification supports recycled parts compliance oversight.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow adjusters to request an audit report for a completed estimate that evaluates the rate and effectiveness of recycled parts incorporation relative to available recycled parts alternatives.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit reports are explicitly required to assess recycled parts usage effectiveness, as described in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow supervisory adjusters to assign a numeric rating (on a defined scale) to a repair facility following the completion of an audit, with the rating and supporting audit outcome stored against the facility's profile.
generated_requirements.rationale: Facility rating by supervisory adjusters is required to inform future facility assignment decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall surface repair facility ratings and associated audit outcomes to adjusters at the point of facility assignment so that historical performance can influence selection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ratings must be actionable; presenting them during assignment closes the feedback loop described in the use case.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) such that collision estimators can only access estimate creation and parts search functions, adjusters can only access claim assignment, estimate review, and audit request functions, and supervisory adjusters additionally have access to facility rating functions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Strict role boundaries are a key requirement to ensure each user type interacts only with functions appropriate to their responsibilities.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall synchronise recycled parts inventory data from the ChoiceParts external data feed at intervals not exceeding 60 minutes, ensuring that estimators work with near-current availability information.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data freshness is critical for accurate parts availability; stale inventory data could lead to estimates referencing unavailable parts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect failures in the ChoiceParts data feed connection and notify affected users with a clear, actionable message within 30 seconds of the failure being detected, while continuing to display the most recently synchronised inventory data with a visible staleness indicator.
generated_requirements.rationale: Graceful degradation and transparent failure handling prevent silent data errors and maintain estimator productivity during outages.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce strict multi-tenant data isolation such that claims, estimates, audit reports, and facility ratings belonging to one insurance company are never accessible to users authenticated under a different insurance company's tenant.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multiple insurance companies share the platform; regulatory and contractual obligations require absolute data segregation between tenants.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users via a secure, token-based mechanism (e.g., OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect) and enforce session expiry after no more than 30 minutes of inactivity.
generated_requirements.rationale: Insurance claims data is sensitive; strong authentication and session management reduce the risk of unauthorised access in shared office and repair shop environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain an immutable audit log recording every create, read, update, and delete action performed on claims, estimates, audit reports, and facility ratings, capturing the user identity, tenant, timestamp, and changed values.
generated_requirements.rationale: Auditability is a key quality attribute; an immutable log supports compliance, dispute resolution, and forensic investigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and deliver a requested audit report within 10 seconds for estimates containing up to 100 line items under normal operating load.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adjusters request audit reports as part of a time-sensitive claims workflow; slow report generation would impede throughput.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall return recycled parts search results to the collision estimator within 3 seconds of query submission under normal operating conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Estimators perform frequent parts searches; slow response times would significantly degrade productivity in both office and repair shop settings.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall be operable on standard web browsers without requiring additional plugin installation, and shall be usable on tablet-sized screens (minimum 768px width) to support use in repair shop environments.
generated_requirements.rationale: The system is used in both office and repair shop environments; browser-based, responsive access ensures usability across device types without IT overhead.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of at least 99.5% measured on a monthly basis, excluding pre-announced maintenance windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: Claims processing is a business-critical operation; downtime directly impacts insurance company operations and customer service levels.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be architected to support the onboarding of a new insurance company tenant through configuration alone, without requiring code changes or redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: A configuration-driven multi-tenancy model reduces onboarding cost and time, and prevents tenant-specific code from accumulating technical debt.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall transmit all data in transit — including ChoiceParts feed ingestion, client-server communication, and inter-service calls — exclusively over TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit protects sensitive claims and parts pricing data from interception, particularly in less-controlled repair shop network environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide collision estimators with a guided workflow that clearly indicates which line items on an estimate have available recycled part alternatives not yet applied, reducing the likelihood of missed recycled part opportunities.
generated_requirements.rationale: Proactive guidance reduces cognitive load and supports the business goal of maximising recycled parts usage without requiring estimators to manually cross-reference all options.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent an estimate from being submitted for adjuster review if the total cost of recycled parts on the estimate causes the overall repair cost to exceed the vehicle's Actual Cash Value (ACV), and shall alert the estimator with a total-loss threshold warning when the repair-to-ACV ratio reaches or exceeds a configurable threshold (default 75%).
generated_requirements.rationale: A fundamental principle in auto insurance claims is that a vehicle should be declared a total loss when repair costs approach or exceed ACV. Failing to enforce this threshold — even when recycled parts reduce costs — exposes the insurer to regulatory violations and bad-faith litigation. The 75% configurable threshold reflects common industry practice (many carriers trigger total-loss review at 70–80% of ACV) and must be tenant-configurable since thresholds vary by carrier and state.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow adjusters to record and store the insured's written consent or documented objection when recycled (non-OEM) parts are specified on an estimate, in accordance with applicable state disclosure requirements, and shall prevent the estimate from advancing to an approved state without this consent record being present.
generated_requirements.rationale: Numerous states (including California, New York, and Texas) legally require that insureds be notified and, in some jurisdictions, provide consent before non-OEM or recycled parts are used in a covered repair. Without capturing this consent within the claims workflow, the insurer risks regulatory fines, DOI complaints, and bad-faith exposure. A generalist engineer would not know this disclosure obligation exists at the estimate-approval gate.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the attachment of one or more photographic or documentary evidence files (e.g., pre-repair photos, post-repair photos, supplement documentation) to a collision repair estimate, with each attachment stored immutably and linked to the specific estimate version to which it was added.
generated_requirements.rationale: Photo documentation is a mandatory component of the claims file in virtually all carrier standard operating procedures and is routinely required during state DOI audits and litigation discovery. Pre- and post-repair photos substantiate the damage scope, validate that recycled parts were appropriate for the damage, and protect the insurer against fraud. Without photo attachment capability, the system cannot serve as the system of record for the full claims file.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall version every estimate such that each revision (supplement) is stored as a distinct, immutable snapshot retaining the full line-item detail, parts list, cost breakdown, and the identity of the user who created the revision, with all prior versions remaining accessible to adjusters and supervisory adjusters.
generated_requirements.rationale: Collision repair estimates routinely go through multiple supplement cycles as hidden damage is discovered during teardown. Insurers are legally required to maintain a complete audit trail of all estimate versions for claims file integrity, litigation support, and state DOI examination. Without immutable versioning, the system cannot demonstrate that cost increases were legitimate supplements rather than retroactive alterations — a critical bad-faith defense requirement.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall apply and display the applicable labor rate for each repair operation on an estimate based on the repair facility's geographic location and the prevailing market labor rate data configured per tenant, and shall flag any labor rate that deviates from the tenant-configured allowable range for that region.
generated_requirements.rationale: Labor rate reasonableness is one of the most frequently disputed elements in collision claims and a primary focus of state DOI market conduct examinations. Insurers are required to pay prevailing competitive labor rates in the repair area (per most state regulations), but also have a duty to control indemnity costs. Without automated labor rate validation against regional benchmarks, the system cannot flag non-compliant estimates before approval, creating both overpayment risk and regulatory exposure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain all claims records, estimates, audit reports, facility ratings, consent records, and associated attachments for a minimum of seven (7) years from the date of claim closure, with tenant-configurable retention periods to accommodate jurisdictions requiring longer retention, and shall prevent deletion of any record within the active retention window.
generated_requirements.rationale: State insurance regulations and carrier reinsurance agreements typically mandate retention of claims files for 5–10 years post-closure. Seven years is the most common minimum across U.S. jurisdictions and aligns with IRS record-keeping requirements that may apply to claim payments. Without enforced retention, the insurer risks regulatory sanctions during DOI market conduct exams and is unable to respond to late-reported litigation or subrogation actions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with or export estimate data in a format compatible with at least one industry-standard estimating data interchange format (e.g., CIECA EMS XML or Mitchell/CCC/Audatex flat-file formats) to support downstream claims management system ingestion and regulatory reporting.
generated_requirements.rationale: The collision repair and insurance industry relies on established data interchange standards (CIECA EMS, Mitchell, CCC ONE, Audatex) for estimate portability between repair shops, insurers, and third-party administrators. Without a standard export format, the system becomes a data silo that cannot feed the insurer's core claims management system (CMS), DRP (Direct Repair Program) management platforms, or state statistical reporting obligations. A generalist engineer would not be aware of these industry-specific interchange standards.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow employees to search for available conference rooms by specifying date, time range, building, floor, capacity, and required equipment, and shall display a list of matching rooms with their availability status in real time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Core actor workflow for employees; room search with multi-criteria filtering is the primary entry point for the reservation process.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow meeting organizers to create a meeting by selecting a conference room, defining a recurring or one-time schedule, attaching an agenda, and inviting internal and external attendees via email invitation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Captures the full meeting-creation workflow specific to the meeting organizer role, including agenda management and attendee invitation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce conflict detection for room reservations, preventing double-booking of the same room for overlapping time slots, and shall immediately notify the organizer with an alternative room suggestion when a conflict is detected.
generated_requirements.rationale: Critical business rule ensuring reservation integrity; conflict resolution guidance reduces organizer friction.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow employees to accept, decline, or propose a new time for meeting invitations, and shall automatically update the meeting organizer and all attendees with the response status.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supports the invitation response workflow for the employee actor and keeps all parties informed of attendance status.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send automated email reminders to all confirmed attendees at configurable intervals before a meeting (e.g., 24 hours and 15 minutes prior), using the organization's configured email server via SMTP or a supported mail protocol.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the notification requirement and the integration touchpoint with external email servers; configurability of intervals supports varying organizational policies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow employees to view an interactive building map for each office location, with conference rooms visually indicated and color-coded by availability status (available, reserved, unavailable) for a selected date and time.
generated_requirements.rationale: Directly addresses the building-map viewing requirement for employees and improves spatial discoverability of rooms.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow meeting organizers to reserve, track, and request transport of associated equipment (e.g., projectors, video-conferencing units) for a meeting, and shall update equipment availability in real time to prevent double-allocation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Captures equipment reservation and transport-request tracking as distinct sub-workflows, with the same conflict-prevention logic applied to physical assets.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow managers to generate reservation reports filtered by building, date range, room, organizer, or department, and to view meeting schedules in daily, weekly, monthly, and agenda list views.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the elevated-access reporting and multi-view scheduling requirements specific to the manager role.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a configurable cancellation policy per organization, including minimum notice periods for cancellation, automatic release of the room and equipment upon cancellation, and notification of all affected attendees.
generated_requirements.rationale: Elicits the business rules around cancellation policies, which vary by organization and have downstream effects on room/equipment availability and attendee communication.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with standard calendar protocols (iCalendar/ICS) so that meeting invitations and updates are automatically reflected in attendees' external calendar applications (e.g., Microsoft Outlook, Google Calendar).
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the calendar-protocol integration touchpoint identified in the elicitation strategy, ensuring interoperability with widely used enterprise calendar tools.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support full multilingual operation, including UI labels, notifications, email templates, and date/time/number formatting, with the ability to add new language packs without modifying application source code.
generated_requirements.rationale: Directly addresses the internationalisation quality attribute and the global, multilingual organization requirement; language-pack extensibility avoids code changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall handle all date and time data in UTC internally and convert to each user's configured local time zone for display, correctly accounting for daylight saving time transitions across all supported regions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timezone correctness is a key internationalisation concern with architectural implications; incorrect timezone handling directly degrades usability for global users.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 500 concurrent authenticated users performing room searches, reservations, and report generation without degradation of response time, with all interactive page loads completing within 3 seconds under this load on standard enterprise hardware.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the scalability quality attribute for large organizations; the 3-second threshold aligns with accepted enterprise usability benchmarks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve a minimum availability of 99.5% measured monthly, excluding scheduled maintenance windows, and shall provide administrators with a mechanism to perform rolling upgrades with zero unplanned downtime.
generated_requirements.rationale: Directly addresses the availability quality attribute; rolling-upgrade capability supports the multi-year lifecycle and minimal administrative overhead goals.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) with at least three distinct roles (Employee, Meeting Organizer, Manager), ensuring that users can only access data and functions permitted by their assigned role, and that all access control decisions are enforced server-side.
generated_requirements.rationale: Role differentiation is central to the use case; server-side enforcement prevents client-side bypass of access restrictions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be fully operable through any modern, standards-compliant web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari — current and one prior major version) without requiring browser plugins or client-side software installation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ensures broad accessibility across enterprise desktop environments and aligns with the browser-accessible, Java-based enterprise application description.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a customer-facing branding configuration interface allowing administrators to customize the application logo, color scheme, fonts, and email template headers without modifying application source code or requiring a redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the white-label/brandable deployment model; runtime configurability of branding eliminates costly redeployment cycles.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose all configurable business rules — including reservation lead times, cancellation notice periods, reminder intervals, maximum booking duration, and room approval workflows — through an administrator configuration interface, with changes taking effect without application restart.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the configurability quality attribute and the multi-tenant deployment model; hot-reloadable configuration minimizes administrative overhead over the multi-year lifecycle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be architected using documented, stable internal APIs separating the presentation, business logic, and data access layers, such that any single layer can be upgraded or replaced independently, and all public interfaces shall be covered by automated regression tests achieving at least 80% code coverage.
generated_requirements.rationale: Directly addresses the maintainability quality attribute and the multi-year lifecycle requirement; layered architecture and test coverage reduce the cost and risk of future changes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support integration with any ANSI SQL-compliant relational database management system (e.g., Oracle, PostgreSQL, Microsoft SQL Server) through a configurable data-source connection, with database vendor selection made at deployment time without code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Addresses the DBMS integration touchpoint and portability across enterprise database environments; vendor-neutral data access reduces customer lock-in.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a configurable maximum advance booking horizon per room category (e.g., standard rooms bookable up to 90 days in advance, executive boardrooms up to 30 days), preventing reservations beyond the defined horizon and displaying the applicable limit to the user at the time of booking.
generated_requirements.rationale: In enterprise facilities management, unbounded advance bookings cause rooms to be monopolized by speculative reservations, starving other employees of access. Tiered booking horizons by room category are a standard operational control used by facilities teams to ensure equitable access and accurate space utilization forecasting.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically release a reserved conference room and notify the organizer and attendees when no check-in is recorded within a configurable grace period (e.g., 15 minutes) after the scheduled meeting start time, using either a physical room panel integration, a mobile check-in action, or a web-based confirmation prompt.
generated_requirements.rationale: No-show room abandonment is one of the most significant sources of wasted space in enterprise offices. Industry data consistently shows 30–40% of booked rooms go unused. An automated no-show release policy (sometimes called 'room recapture') is a core facilities management control that directly improves utilization rates and is expected by enterprise customers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record and retain a complete, tamper-evident audit log of all reservation lifecycle events — including creation, modification, cancellation, check-in, no-show release, and administrative overrides — capturing the acting user identity, timestamp, changed field values (before and after), and source IP address. Audit logs shall be retained for a minimum of 12 months and shall be exportable by administrators in a structured format (e.g., CSV or JSON).
generated_requirements.rationale: Enterprise organizations are subject to internal audit, ISO 27001, SOX, and data governance requirements that mandate demonstrable records of who booked what space and when, and that records cannot be silently altered. Facilities managers are routinely asked to produce booking histories for dispute resolution, cost chargebacks, and compliance audits. A 12-month retention period aligns with common annual audit cycles.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a room approval workflow for designated rooms (e.g., executive boardrooms, external client suites), requiring a nominated approver (e.g., an executive assistant or facilities coordinator) to explicitly approve or reject a reservation request before it is confirmed, with configurable approval timeout behavior (auto-approve or auto-reject) and full notification to the requestor at each workflow state transition.
generated_requirements.rationale: High-value or restricted rooms in enterprise environments are not self-service; they require human gatekeeping to enforce protocol, manage VIP scheduling conflicts, and ensure appropriate use. Without an approval workflow, any employee can book a boardroom intended for executive or client use, which is a common facilities governance gap that creates operational and reputational risk.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall capture and report space utilization metrics per room, including actual occupancy rate (based on check-in data), booking utilization rate (booked hours vs. available hours), no-show rate, and peak demand periods, aggregated by building, floor, room category, and department, and shall make these metrics available via a dedicated analytics dashboard and as scheduled exportable reports.
generated_requirements.rationale: Space utilization data is the primary input for facilities capital planning decisions — including real estate lease renewals, office reconfiguration, and headcount capacity planning. Without structured utilization reporting, facilities managers cannot justify space investments or reductions to finance and leadership. This is a core deliverable expected from any enterprise-grade workplace management system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the designation of rooms as temporarily or permanently out of service by facilities administrators, with a mandatory reason code and optional free-text note (e.g., maintenance, deep cleaning, refurbishment, health and safety hold), automatically blocking all new bookings for the affected period, cancelling any existing reservations with attendee notification, and displaying the out-of-service status on the building map and search results.
generated_requirements.rationale: Facilities teams must be able to take rooms offline for planned and unplanned maintenance, cleaning cycles, safety inspections, or refurbishment without manual intervention in individual bookings. This is a routine operational requirement; without it, maintenance crews arrive to find rooms occupied, or employees book rooms that are physically unavailable, creating safety and service delivery risks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support cost-centre or department chargeback reporting by associating each reservation with the organizer's cost centre (sourced from the integrated HR/directory system or entered manually), calculating a notional or actual room cost based on configurable per-room hourly rates, and producing chargeback reports filterable by cost centre, department, building, and date range for export to finance systems.
generated_requirements.rationale: Many enterprise organizations operate internal facilities chargeback models where business units are billed for the space they consume, particularly for premium rooms or external client facilities. Without chargeback reporting, facilities costs are invisible to department heads, removing the financial incentive to release unused bookings. This is a standard requirement in enterprise real estate and facilities management.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce configurable room booking quotas per user or per department over a rolling time window (e.g., a maximum of 10 room-hours per user per week, or a maximum of 3 simultaneous active reservations per user), with quota thresholds and enforcement scope (user-level, department-level, or both) fully configurable by administrators without code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Without booking quotas, power users or entire departments can monopolize room inventory, particularly in space-constrained offices. Quota enforcement is a standard fairness and access-equity control in enterprise facilities management, and is especially critical during periods of high demand such as end-of-quarter planning cycles or return-to-office transitions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the organization's authoritative HR or identity directory (e.g., Microsoft Active Directory, LDAP, or an HR system via SCIM 2.0) to automatically provision, update, and deprovision user accounts and their associated attributes (name, department, cost centre, manager, office location) in near real time, ensuring that terminated employees are immediately denied access and that role assignments reflect current organizational structure.
generated_requirements.rationale: Stale user accounts are a critical security and compliance risk in enterprise systems. Terminated employees retaining active booking accounts can create unauthorized facility access records and audit failures. Automated deprovisioning via directory integration is a baseline requirement for ISO 27001 and SOC 2 compliance and is expected by enterprise IT security teams as a condition of deployment approval.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the configuration of room-specific health, safety, and compliance attributes — including maximum permitted occupancy (distinct from seating capacity), accessibility features (e.g., wheelchair access, hearing loop, adjustable lighting), and applicable safety certifications or inspection expiry dates — and shall enforce maximum occupancy limits by preventing reservations that specify an attendee count exceeding the room's permitted occupancy, displaying a compliance warning to the organizer.
generated_requirements.rationale: Maximum occupancy limits are a legal requirement under fire safety and building codes (e.g., NFPA 101, local fire regulations) and are distinct from the physical seating count. Facilities managers are legally accountable for ensuring rooms are not booked beyond their permitted occupancy. Accessibility attribute visibility is required under ADA/Equality Act obligations to ensure employees with disabilities can identify suitable rooms. These are non-negotiable compliance concerns that a generalist engineer would typically model only as informational fields rather than enforced constraints.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically generate an Inventory Quantity Adjustment (IQA) document at the end of each business day by consuming the day's POS sales data and applying the corresponding product formulas to calculate ingredient consumption quantities.
generated_requirements.rationale: This is the core functional outcome of the platform — automating IQA generation from sales data and formulas is the primary business driver.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall decompose semi-product ingredients within a product formula recursively until all leaf-level raw ingredients are resolved, prior to calculating inventory adjustments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Semi-product decomposition is a critical business rule; failure to fully resolve nested components would produce incorrect inventory deductions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall apply ingredient substitution rules defined in a product formula when the primary ingredient's current inventory level falls below the required quantity, automatically selecting the highest-priority available substitute before generating the IQA document.
generated_requirements.rationale: Substitution logic is a key differentiator of the platform; it must be evaluated against live inventory levels to produce accurate and actionable adjustment documents.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Formula Administrators to create, edit, version, and deactivate product formulas, where each formula version is immutably stored with a timestamp and the identity of the modifying user.
generated_requirements.rationale: Formula versioning with immutable history supports both operational accuracy (using the correct formula for a given sales date) and audit compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Formula Administrators to define ingredient substitution entries within a product formula, specifying the substitute ingredient, substitution ratio, and priority order, with a minimum of one and a maximum of ten substitutes per primary ingredient.
generated_requirements.rationale: Structured substitution definitions with ratios and priorities are required to enable deterministic, rule-based substitution during IQA generation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized staff to build and organize product menus in a hierarchical (parent–child category) structure and associate each menu item with a product formula, enabling synchronization of the menu hierarchy with connected POS terminals.
generated_requirements.rationale: Hierarchical menu management with POS integration is a stated platform capability required by POS operators and formula administrators.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC) with at least three distinct roles — Inventory Manager, Formula Administrator, and POS Operator — where each role is granted access only to the modules, data, and actions explicitly permitted for that role.
generated_requirements.rationale: RBAC is a hard requirement to prevent unauthorized cross-functional data access and to satisfy audit-compliance obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall record a tamper-evident audit log entry for every create, update, delete, and status-change operation on formulas, menus, inventory adjustments, and user accounts, capturing the actor's identity, timestamp, affected record identifier, and before/after field values.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit-compliant accounting standards require a complete, immutable trail of all data-modifying operations for regulatory and internal review purposes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Inventory Managers to manually review, annotate, and approve or reject a generated IQA document before it is posted to the inventory ledger, with rejected documents requiring a mandatory rejection reason.
generated_requirements.rationale: A human approval gate ensures data integrity and gives inventory managers control over postings, satisfying accounting oversight requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete the end-of-day IQA document generation process — including semi-product decomposition, substitution evaluation, and document creation — within 60 seconds for a sales dataset containing up to 10,000 line items.
generated_requirements.rationale: A time-bound generation process ensures the IQA is available for manager review before the next business day's operations begin, preventing operational bottlenecks.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 20 concurrent authenticated users performing read and write operations across all modules without degradation of response time beyond 3 seconds for any individual UI interaction.
generated_requirements.rationale: Restaurant back-office environments involve multiple simultaneous users; concurrency support is essential to avoid workflow contention during peak hours.

generated_requirements.text: All user passwords shall be stored using a salted cryptographic hash (minimum bcrypt cost factor 12 or equivalent), and all data transmitted between client workstations and the server shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Credential and data-in-transit protection are baseline security controls required to protect sensitive business and accounting data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce session timeouts, automatically locking an inactive user session after no more than 15 minutes of inactivity and requiring re-authentication before access is restored.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout controls reduce the risk of unauthorized access on shared or unattended workstations common in restaurant environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall present each user role with a tailored interface that surfaces only the modules, actions, and data fields relevant to that role, hiding or disabling all controls outside the role's permission scope.
generated_requirements.rationale: A role-contextual UI reduces cognitive load, minimizes operator error, and prevents accidental exposure of sensitive controls to unauthorized roles.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an application availability of at least 99.5% during defined business hours (06:00–24:00 local time), excluding pre-scheduled maintenance windows communicated at least 24 hours in advance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Restaurant operations are time-critical; system downtime during business hours directly impacts sales recording, inventory accuracy, and POS functionality.

generated_requirements.text: In the event of a failure during IQA document generation (e.g., POS data feed interruption or database error), the system shall roll back the partial generation atomically, log the failure with full diagnostic context, and notify the Inventory Manager via an in-application alert.
generated_requirements.rationale: Partial or corrupt IQA documents would cause incorrect inventory postings; atomic rollback and alerting ensure data integrity and prompt human intervention.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable and fully operational on existing Windows 10 or Windows 11 workstations and Windows Server 2016 or later server infrastructure without requiring hardware upgrades.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly constrains the solution to the existing Windows-based hardware environment, making OS and hardware compatibility a hard boundary.

generated_requirements.text: The system's business rules for IQA generation — including substitution priority logic, semi-product decomposition depth limit, and formula version selection strategy — shall be configurable by a system administrator through a dedicated configuration interface without requiring code changes or redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Externalizing business rules reduces the cost and risk of adapting the system to evolving restaurant operations and accounting policies over time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a complete, point-in-time recoverable backup of all transactional data (formulas, menus, IQA documents, audit logs) with a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) of no more than 1 hour and a Recovery Time Objective (RTO) of no more than 4 hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accounting and inventory data are business-critical; defined RPO/RTO targets ensure the organization can recover from failures without significant data loss or prolonged downtime.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be architected with a modular, layered codebase such that the POS data integration module, formula engine, and inventory ledger module can each be independently updated, tested, and deployed without requiring changes to the other modules.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular separation of concerns reduces the blast radius of changes, accelerates testing cycles, and supports long-term evolution of the platform as business needs grow.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the definition and tracking of unit-of-measure (UOM) conversions for each ingredient (e.g., kg to g, liter to ml, case to unit), and shall automatically apply the correct conversion factor when calculating ingredient consumption quantities during IQA generation, flagging any formula line item whose UOM does not match the inventory ledger UOM for manual review.
generated_requirements.rationale: Restaurants purchase ingredients in bulk units (cases, liters, kg) but consume them in recipe units (grams, ml, pieces). A mismatch between purchase UOM and recipe UOM is one of the most common sources of inventory variance in food service operations. Without enforced UOM conversion, IQA documents will systematically over- or under-report consumption, leading to phantom stock or false shortages.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow Inventory Managers to record and associate a supplier and supplier SKU with each ingredient, and shall generate a low-stock alert — delivered via in-application notification and optionally via email — when an ingredient's on-hand quantity falls below a configurable par level, identifying the preferred supplier and the quantity required to return to par.
generated_requirements.rationale: Par-level management and supplier linkage are foundational to restaurant inventory control. Without automated low-stock alerts tied to supplier information, operations staff must manually monitor stock levels, increasing the risk of ingredient stockouts that force mid-service menu 86ing (item unavailability). Linking alerts to supplier SKUs also accelerates the purchase order workflow, which is a direct downstream dependency of the IQA process.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the recording of physical stock count results against each ingredient, calculate the variance between the physical count and the system's theoretical on-hand quantity (derived from IQA postings), and present a variance report that categorizes discrepancies by magnitude and flags items exceeding a configurable acceptable variance threshold (e.g., ±3% by value) for investigation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Physical inventory counts (weekly, monthly, or period-end) are a mandatory control in food service operations and are required by most franchise agreements and food cost audit standards. The gap between theoretical inventory (calculated from sales and formulas) and physical count reveals shrinkage, theft, over-portioning, and formula inaccuracies. Without a built-in variance analysis tool, the IQA system's outputs cannot be validated, undermining the entire inventory control process.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a minimum shelf-life or expiry date field for perishable ingredients, and shall generate an expiry alert when an ingredient lot's remaining shelf life falls below a configurable threshold (e.g., 2 days), preventing expired ingredient lots from being selected as available stock during substitution evaluation or IQA consumption calculations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Food safety regulations (including FDA Food Safety Modernization Act and equivalent local health codes) require that expired or out-of-date ingredients are not used in food preparation. Allowing the system to count expired stock as available inventory during substitution evaluation could result in regulatory violations, health code breaches, and liability exposure. Expiry tracking is also required for FIFO (First-In, First-Out) stock rotation, which is a standard food safety practice.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the association of allergen declarations (e.g., gluten, dairy, nuts, soy, shellfish, eggs — per applicable regional food labeling regulations) with each ingredient, and shall automatically propagate and display the aggregated allergen profile of a product formula whenever a formula is created, modified, or when an ingredient substitution is applied, alerting Formula Administrators if a substitution introduces a new allergen not present in the original formula.
generated_requirements.rationale: Allergen mislabeling is a life-safety and legal liability issue in food service. Regulatory frameworks such as the EU Food Information to Consumers Regulation (EU FIC 1169/2011) and the US FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act mandate accurate allergen disclosure. Critically, the system's ingredient substitution feature poses a specific risk: an automatically selected substitute could introduce an undisclosed allergen into a dish, which could cause a severe allergic reaction in a customer. This is a domain-specific edge case that a generalist engineer would almost certainly overlook.

generated_requirements.text: During registration, the system shall present the customer with a choice between two subscription plans: (1) Pay-As-You-Go, where the customer pays per individual movie title, and (2) Unlimited Subscription, where the customer pays a recurring fee for unrestricted access to the full catalog.
generated_requirements.rationale: The two distinct subscription models are a core business differentiator and must be explicitly selectable at onboarding.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a two-day (48-hour) viewing window for each movie purchased or unlocked under the Pay-As-You-Go plan, automatically revoking streaming access once the window expires from the time of first play.
generated_requirements.rationale: The two-day viewing window is a critical business-logic and access-control rule that protects content licensing agreements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to browse the movie catalog by category (e.g., genre, country of origin, language, release year) and to search for movies by title, actor, director, or keyword, returning results within 2 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Discoverability through browsing and search is essential to the customer journey and directly impacts engagement and conversion.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall accept payment for movie purchases and subscription fees via credit/debit card (Visa, Mastercard) and pre-paid cards, processing transactions through a PCI-DSS-compliant payment gateway.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supporting both card types addresses the payment preferences of Nigerian and broader African audiences, including the unbanked segment that relies on pre-paid cards.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to activate, deactivate, and track the redemption status of pre-paid cards, including setting card denominations and expiry dates.
generated_requirements.rationale: Pre-paid card lifecycle management is an explicit administrator responsibility and must be fully supported to prevent fraud and revenue leakage.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow administrators to add, edit, and remove movie titles in the catalog, including uploading metadata (title, synopsis, cast, director, genre, language, release year, poster image, and trailer) and the video file itself.
generated_requirements.rationale: Full catalog management capability is required for administrators to keep content current and accurately described.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authenticated customers to submit a star rating (1–5) and a written review for any movie they have watched, and shall allow administrators to moderate (approve, edit, or remove) reviews before or after publication.
generated_requirements.rationale: Community reviews drive engagement and cultural resonance; moderation protects the platform from abusive or inappropriate content.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide the Izogn Manager with a real-time analytics dashboard displaying key metrics including total sales revenue, active subscriptions by plan type, most-watched movies, geographic distribution of viewers, and pre-paid card redemption rates.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Manager role requires visibility into sales and usage data to make informed business decisions; real-time data maximises its value.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to manage their account, including updating personal details, changing their subscription plan, viewing purchase and viewing history, and deleting their account.
generated_requirements.rationale: Personalised account management is an explicit platform feature and supports customer autonomy and data-privacy compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall stream video content using adaptive bitrate (ABR) streaming, automatically adjusting video quality between a minimum of 360p and a maximum of 1080p based on the customer's available bandwidth, with initial playback beginning within 5 seconds of the play action.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adaptive streaming is essential for African markets where internet connectivity varies widely; the 5-second start threshold sets a measurable quality-of-experience baseline.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be available 99.5% of the time on a monthly basis, excluding scheduled maintenance windows that must be communicated to users at least 24 hours in advance.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is critical for a streaming platform where downtime directly translates to lost revenue and customer dissatisfaction.

generated_requirements.text: All customer passwords shall be stored using a strong adaptive hashing algorithm (e.g., bcrypt with a minimum cost factor of 12), and all data transmitted between the client and server shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher.
generated_requirements.rationale: Protecting customer credentials and payment data in transit is a fundamental security requirement and a prerequisite for regulatory compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement role-based access control (RBAC) with at least three distinct roles — Customer, Administrator, and Manager — ensuring that each role can only access the features and data explicitly assigned to it.
generated_requirements.rationale: RBAC prevents privilege escalation and ensures that sensitive administrative and financial functions are not accessible to regular customers.

generated_requirements.text: The platform's user interface shall display prices in Nigerian Naira (NGN) by default, with the ability to switch to USD and GBP, and shall use culturally relevant imagery, colour palettes, and featured content that reflect African — particularly Nigerian — cinema and aesthetics.
generated_requirements.rationale: Cultural resonance and local currency display are key differentiators for the target Nigerian and African audience, directly impacting trust and conversion.

generated_requirements.text: The web application shall conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility guidelines, including sufficient colour contrast ratios, keyboard navigability, screen-reader-compatible ARIA labels, and captions/subtitles available for all video content.
generated_requirements.rationale: Accessibility compliance ensures the platform is inclusive to users with disabilities and supports the goal of welcoming a broad global user base.

generated_requirements.text: The system architecture shall support horizontal scaling to handle a minimum of 10,000 concurrent streaming sessions without degradation in video start time or buffering rate, using a cloud-based content delivery network (CDN) with Points of Presence (PoPs) in West Africa, Europe, and North America.
generated_requirements.rationale: Scalability via CDN is essential to serve a geographically distributed audience with low latency, especially during peak events such as new movie releases.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support content localisation by allowing movie metadata (titles, synopses, and UI labels) to be provided in English, Yoruba, Igbo, and Hausa, with English as the default language and a user-selectable language preference stored in the account profile.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-language support for Nigeria's major languages is critical for cultural resonance and broadening the accessible user base beyond English speakers.

generated_requirements.text: The system codebase shall follow a modular, documented architecture with at least 80% unit-test coverage on business-logic modules (subscription enforcement, payment processing, access-window management), and all APIs shall be versioned to allow backward-compatible updates.
generated_requirements.rationale: High test coverage and API versioning reduce regression risk and enable the platform to evolve its subscription and payment logic without breaking existing integrations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement automated daily backups of all customer data, transaction records, and the movie catalog database, with a recovery point objective (RPO) of 24 hours and a recovery time objective (RTO) of 4 hours in the event of a system failure.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defined RPO and RTO targets ensure business continuity and protect against data loss for a platform handling financial transactions and user-generated content.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall store and enforce per-title territorial licensing rights, preventing customers in unlicensed countries or regions from accessing, purchasing, or streaming any movie title for which the platform does not hold a valid distribution licence, and shall display a user-friendly geo-restriction notice when access is blocked.
generated_requirements.rationale: African content — especially Nollywood titles — is frequently licensed on a territory-by-territory basis. Distributors and rights-holders (e.g., EbonyLife, Inkblot, FilmOne) grant streaming rights per region. Ignoring geo-licensing enforcement exposes the platform to copyright infringement claims and licence revocation, a critical risk a generalist engineer would overlook.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with at least one Nigerian/African-native mobile money or instant payment channel — specifically USSD-based payment (e.g., via Interswitch, Flutterwave, or Paystack) and bank transfer via the NIBSS Instant Payment (NIP) scheme — as first-class payment methods alongside card payments, with real-time transaction confirmation and automatic subscription or purchase activation upon settlement.
generated_requirements.rationale: A significant portion of the Nigerian target audience is underbanked or prefers local payment rails over international card schemes. USSD payments work on feature phones without internet access, and NIP bank transfers are the dominant payment method for many Nigerian consumers. Omitting these channels would severely limit addressable market penetration — a critical oversight for an Africa-first platform.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with the Nigeria Data Protection Act 2023 (NDPA) and its implementing regulations, including: obtaining explicit, informed consent before collecting personal data; providing customers with the right to access, correct, and delete their personal data; appointing a Data Protection Officer (DPO) where required; and submitting annual data protection audit reports to the Nigeria Data Protection Commission (NDPC).
generated_requirements.rationale: The NDPA 2023 is Nigeria's primary data protection law, replacing the NDPR 2019, and carries significant penalties for non-compliance. Any platform collecting personal data from Nigerian residents — including registration details, payment data, and viewing history — is legally obligated to comply. A generalist engineer focused on GDPR or generic privacy principles would likely miss Nigeria-specific regulatory obligations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement a Digital Rights Management (DRM) solution — supporting at minimum Widevine (for Chrome/Android) and FairPlay (for Safari/iOS) — to encrypt all premium video streams, prevent unauthorised downloading or screen-capture of protected content, and enforce licence token expiry aligned with the 48-hour Pay-As-You-Go viewing window.
generated_requirements.rationale: Nollywood and African content piracy is rampant, with titles frequently appearing on YouTube, Telegram, and piracy sites within hours of release. Rights-holders and distributors increasingly require DRM as a contractual condition for licensing content to OTT platforms. Without DRM, the platform risks losing content licensing deals and violating distribution agreements — a domain-critical concern invisible to a generalist engineer.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall comply with the National Broadcasting Commission (NBC) Nigeria content regulations and the Nigerian Copyright Act 2022, including ensuring that all hosted content has verifiable chain-of-title documentation uploaded and stored against each title record, and that the platform does not host content that violates NBC content classification guidelines (e.g., age-rating restrictions for explicit material).
generated_requirements.rationale: The NBC regulates broadcast and streaming content in Nigeria, and the Nigerian Copyright Act 2022 modernised IP enforcement including for digital platforms. Failure to maintain chain-of-title documentation is a leading cause of licensing disputes in the Nollywood ecosystem. Age-rating non-compliance can result in regulatory sanctions. These are Africa-specific legal obligations a generalist engineer would not know to include.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall gracefully handle low-bandwidth and high-latency network conditions prevalent in sub-Saharan Africa by: (1) offering a 'data-saver' streaming mode capped at 144p/240p for users who opt in; (2) implementing aggressive client-side video segment pre-buffering during periods of stable connectivity; and (3) displaying estimated data consumption per hour at each quality tier before the user initiates playback.
generated_requirements.rationale: Average fixed and mobile broadband speeds in Nigeria and much of West Africa remain well below global averages, and many users are on metered mobile data plans. Platforms like Showmax and Netflix Africa have introduced data-saver modes specifically for this market. Without these features, the platform will suffer high abandonment rates and poor user experience for its core audience — a market-specific technical concern beyond a generalist's frame of reference.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement a pre-paid card fraud prevention mechanism that rate-limits redemption attempts per IP address and per account (maximum 5 failed attempts within a 10-minute window before a temporary lockout), detects and flags bulk sequential card code entry patterns indicative of card-cracking attacks, and logs all redemption attempts with timestamps and IP geolocation for administrator audit review.
generated_requirements.rationale: Pre-paid/voucher cards are a primary payment channel in the Nigerian market (sold at kiosks, supermarkets, and via agents), making them a high-value fraud target. Card-cracking — automated sequential guessing of voucher codes — is a well-documented attack vector against African OTT and airtime voucher systems. The existing requirements describe pre-paid card management but include no fraud controls, a significant security gap in this market context.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a Nigerian Consumer Protection Council (CPC)-compliant refund and dispute resolution policy at the point of purchase, and shall provide customers with a self-service mechanism to raise a payment dispute or request a refund for failed or duplicate transactions, with a mandatory resolution SLA of 5 business days and automated status notifications to the customer at each stage of the dispute process.
generated_requirements.rationale: The Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Act (FCCPA) 2018 and the Consumer Protection Council mandate clear refund and dispute resolution processes for digital commerce platforms operating in Nigeria. Failed payment transactions are disproportionately common on Nigerian payment infrastructure due to network issues and bank gateway timeouts, making a self-service dispute mechanism operationally essential — not just a regulatory nicety.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall receive incoming lead submissions from external vendors via a RESTful web service API, accepting payloads in JSON or XML format over HTTPS.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defines the primary ingestion interface for vendor-submitted leads, establishing the integration contract at the system boundary.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate each incoming lead against a configurable set of required fields (e.g., first name, last name, email address, phone number, program of interest) and reject any lead that fails field-level validation, returning a structured error response identifying each failed field and the reason for rejection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ensures data integrity at the point of ingestion before scoring logic is applied, and provides actionable feedback to vendors.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall compute a composite lead score for each valid lead by evaluating a weighted combination of contact criteria (e.g., phone reachability, email validity, geographic location) and academic criteria (e.g., program interest alignment, education level, enrollment timeline), where individual criterion weights and score thresholds are configurable at both the corporate and school levels.
generated_requirements.rationale: Captures the core scoring model that drives all downstream disposition decisions, with configurability at multiple organizational levels.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically forward leads whose composite score meets or exceeds the configured high-score threshold to the enterprise system via its published integration API, triggering auto-assignment to an available admissions representative within the same transaction.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defines the auto-accept disposition path and its integration touchpoint with the enterprise system, including the trigger condition for representative assignment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall place leads whose composite score falls within the configured mid-range threshold band into a manual review queue, making them visible and actionable to Enrollment Coordinators through the administrative interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defines the manual review disposition path and ensures mid-range leads are surfaced to the appropriate human reviewers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall reject leads whose composite score falls below the configured low-score threshold or that fail validation, and shall return a structured rejection response to the originating vendor that includes a machine-readable rejection code and a human-readable reason for each rejection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defines the rejection disposition path and ensures vendors receive sufficient information to act on or dispute the rejection.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect duplicate leads by comparing incoming lead records against existing leads using a configurable matching algorithm (e.g., matching on email address, phone number, or a combination of name and program), flag identified duplicates with a duplicate status, and store them for reference without forwarding them to the enterprise system.
generated_requirements.rationale: Prevents duplicate leads from polluting the enterprise system and ensures vendors are not credited for leads already in the pipeline.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enable vendors whose leads have been rejected to submit credit validation records to the enterprise billing system via a dedicated API endpoint, transmitting the original lead reference ID, rejection code, and vendor identifier as part of the credit request payload.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supports the vendor billing reconciliation workflow by providing a formal mechanism for credit dispute submission.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a secured administrative interface through which authorized Administrative users can create, update, and deactivate corporate-level and school-level scoring parameters, criterion weights, and composite score threshold values, with all changes taking effect on subsequently processed leads without requiring a system restart.
generated_requirements.rationale: Enables runtime configurability of the scoring model by administrators without engineering intervention, supporting business agility.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide Administrative users with access to defect reports through the administrative interface, including reports on rejected leads, duplicate leads, validation failures, and integration errors, filterable by date range, vendor, school, and rejection reason.
generated_requirements.rationale: Supports operational oversight and quality monitoring by giving administrators visibility into system defects and lead processing anomalies.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control (RBAC), restricting access to the administrative interface and parameter management functions to users assigned the Administrator role, and restricting manual review queue access to users assigned the Enrollment Coordinator role, with all role assignments managed through the enterprise identity provider.
generated_requirements.rationale: Prevents unauthorized access to sensitive configuration and lead data by enforcing least-privilege access aligned to organizational roles.
