generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce two distinct user roles — Commissioner and Team Owner — each with a separate, non-overlapping set of permissions that are validated on every authenticated request.
generated_requirements.rationale: Role-based access control is critical to prevent team owners from executing privileged governance actions reserved for the commissioner.

generated_requirements.text: The commissioner shall be able to create, edit, and delete league-wide settings including scoring rules, roster size limits, trade deadlines, waiver wire order, and playoff bracket configuration.
generated_requirements.rationale: Commissioners require full governance control over league configuration to administer the league throughout the NFL season.

generated_requirements.text: A team owner shall be able to add a free-agent NFL player to their roster, subject to roster size limits and waiver wire rules configured by the commissioner.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adding players is a core roster management transaction that must respect league-defined constraints to preserve competitive integrity.

generated_requirements.text: A team owner shall be able to drop an NFL player from their roster, immediately returning that player to the free-agent pool or waiver wire as configured by the commissioner.
generated_requirements.rationale: Dropping players is a fundamental roster transaction; the disposition of the dropped player must follow league rules to ensure fairness.

generated_requirements.text: Team owners shall be able to propose, accept, reject, and cancel trades of one or more NFL players between two teams, with the commissioner able to veto or approve any pending trade.
generated_requirements.rationale: Trade workflows require multi-party state management and commissioner oversight to prevent collusion and enforce league governance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display up-to-date NFL player statistics and calculated fantasy points on player profile and roster pages, refreshing data at intervals no greater than 60 seconds during live game windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time statistics are a primary decision-making tool for team owners; stale data during live games degrades the core user experience.

generated_requirements.text: Player statistics and fantasy point data displayed to users shall be refreshed from the upstream NFL data feed with a maximum end-to-end latency of 60 seconds during active game windows (Sunday 1 PM – 11 PM ET, Monday and Thursday nights).
generated_requirements.rationale: Low-latency data refresh is a key quality attribute; delays beyond 60 seconds materially impact roster decisions during live games.

generated_requirements.text: The platform shall maintain a minimum availability of 99.9% (measured monthly), with planned maintenance windows scheduled exclusively outside of peak NFL game windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is explicitly called out as a core quality attribute; downtime during live games would prevent users from completing time-sensitive roster transactions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 10,000 concurrent authenticated users without degradation of response times, scaling horizontally to handle peak loads during Sunday NFL game windows.
generated_requirements.rationale: Concurrent user load during peak NFL windows is a primary scalability risk; the platform must be architected to scale without performance degradation.

generated_requirements.text: All roster transaction operations (add, drop, trade acceptance) shall complete and be confirmed to the user within 3 seconds under normal load conditions.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid response times for transactions are a stated quality attribute; slow confirmations erode user trust and may cause duplicate submissions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall guarantee that each roster transaction is processed exactly once, using atomic database operations or distributed locking to prevent duplicate adds, drops, or trade executions under concurrent requests.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data integrity of roster state is critical; duplicate or partial transactions would corrupt standings and violate league rules.

generated_requirements.text: The league page shall display a message board showing the most recent community posts, with each post attributed to its author, timestamp, and team name, sorted in reverse chronological order.
generated_requirements.rationale: The message board is an explicit engagement feature; attribution and chronological ordering are baseline usability requirements for community interaction.

generated_requirements.text: The commissioner shall be able to delete any message board post and permanently ban a team owner from posting, with all moderation actions logged with a timestamp and the commissioner's user ID.
generated_requirements.rationale: Commissioner moderation privileges are necessary for community governance; audit logging ensures accountability for moderation decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall send an in-app notification and an optional email notification to a team owner within 5 minutes when a trade is proposed to them, when a trade they proposed is accepted or rejected, or when the commissioner vetoes a trade.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely notifications for trade state changes are essential to keep participants engaged and ensure time-sensitive decisions are not missed.

generated_requirements.text: The application's primary navigation shall allow a user to move between the league page and any team page within the league in no more than two clicks or taps from any screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Intuitive navigation is explicitly cited in the use case; a two-click maximum enforces a measurable standard for navigational efficiency.

generated_requirements.text: All user-facing pages shall achieve a Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) of under 2.5 seconds and a Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) score below 0.1, as measured by Google Core Web Vitals under a simulated 4G connection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Page load performance directly impacts perceived usability; Core Web Vitals provide industry-standard, measurable thresholds for a responsive experience.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the client and server shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher, and all passwords shall be stored using an adaptive hashing algorithm (e.g., bcrypt with a minimum cost factor of 12).
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption in transit and secure credential storage are baseline security requirements to protect user accounts and sensitive league data.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate users via username/password with support for multi-factor authentication (MFA), and shall automatically invalidate sessions after 30 minutes of inactivity.
generated_requirements.rationale: MFA and session timeout policies reduce the risk of unauthorized account access, particularly important given the role-based privilege model.

generated_requirements.text: The application codebase shall maintain a minimum of 80% unit and integration test coverage, and all modules shall adhere to a documented coding standard to facilitate ongoing maintenance and feature additions.
generated_requirements.rationale: A seasonal sports platform requires frequent updates (e.g., rule changes, new NFL seasons); high test coverage and coding standards reduce regression risk and onboarding time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a full audit log of all commissioner actions (settings changes, trade vetoes, post deletions, bans) and all roster transactions (adds, drops, trade executions), retaining records for a minimum of two NFL seasons.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit trails support dispute resolution, accountability, and data integrity verification — all critical in a competitive league management context.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display a mandatory attribution notice on all pages rendering NFL player statistics, names, team affiliations, and logos, identifying the official NFL data licensor by name and conforming to the attribution and branding guidelines specified in the data licensing agreement, including any required trademark symbols.
generated_requirements.rationale: NFL data licensing agreements (e.g., with Sportradar, Stats Perform, or the NFL's own data feed) universally require licensees to display specific attribution language and approved branding. Failure to comply can result in license termination or financial penalties. A generalist engineer would not know to encode these contractual obligations as system requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a hard roster transaction lock for each team during the period beginning when the NFL's official weekly waiver wire processing window opens and ending when it closes, preventing any add, drop, or trade acceptance that would alter a roster until the waiver wire results are published and applied. The exact lock window shall be configurable by the commissioner to match the NFL weekly schedule.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fantasy football platforms must respect the NFL's weekly waiver wire cycle (typically Tuesday night through Wednesday morning). Allowing transactions during the processing window creates race conditions where a player could be simultaneously awarded via waiver and added via free agency, corrupting roster integrity. This is a well-known operational edge case in fantasy league management that non-domain engineers routinely overlook.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ingest and reflect official NFL injury designations (Questionable, Doubtful, Out, IR, PUP, NFI, Suspended) on player profile and roster pages within 15 minutes of the designation being published in the upstream NFL data feed, and shall visually flag any player on a team's active roster who carries an 'Out', 'IR', 'PUP', 'NFI', or 'Suspended' designation at the time the weekly lineup lock occurs.
generated_requirements.rationale: Injury and roster status designations are among the most consequential data points in fantasy football decision-making. The NFL publishes official injury reports Wednesday through Friday and a final injury report on game day. Surfacing stale or missing designations — especially at lineup lock — directly harms team owners and is a leading source of user complaints and disputes in fantasy platforms. The specific designation codes (IR, PUP, NFI, Suspended) also carry different transaction eligibility rules that the system must enforce.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a weekly starting lineup lock per team on a position-by-position basis, preventing changes to any player's starting lineup slot once the NFL game in which that player is scheduled to participate has kicked off, while continuing to allow lineup changes for players whose games have not yet started within the same week's slate.
generated_requirements.rationale: Lineup lock is a foundational integrity rule in fantasy football: owners must not be able to swap in a player after their game has already started (e.g., benching a player who had a fumble in the first quarter). The lock must be per-player and per-game-kickoff, not a single weekly lock, because the NFL schedule includes Thursday Night, Sunday morning, Sunday afternoon, Sunday Night, and Monday Night games all within the same scoring week. This granular, game-kickoff-driven lock is a domain-specific requirement that generalist engineers consistently miss.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support commissioner-configurable scoring rule parameters for all standard and advanced NFL statistical categories, including but not limited to: passing yards, passing touchdowns, interceptions thrown, rushing yards, rushing touchdowns, receiving yards, receiving touchdowns, receptions (PPR), two-point conversions, fumbles lost, return touchdowns, sacks, defensive touchdowns, and special teams touchdowns. Each scoring parameter shall accept fractional point values and negative point values.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fantasy football leagues operate under a wide variety of scoring formats — standard, PPR (points per reception), half-PPR, IDP (individual defensive players), and custom hybrid formats. The existing requirement only references 'scoring rules' generically. A domain specialist knows that the system must explicitly support the full taxonomy of NFL statistical events that map to fantasy points, including fractional and negative values (e.g., -2 for a fumble lost, 0.1 per receiving yard), or the platform will be unable to serve the majority of competitive leagues.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall obtain and record affirmative age verification consent from all users during registration, and shall prohibit account creation and platform access for any user who self-declares or is determined to be under 13 years of age, in compliance with the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA). For users in jurisdictions subject to additional age-gating requirements (e.g., GDPR Article 8 for EU residents under 16), the system shall apply the applicable minimum age threshold.
generated_requirements.rationale: Fantasy sports platforms that collect personal data (email, team name, payment info for paid leagues) are subject to COPPA in the US and GDPR in the EU. The NFL's own licensing terms and major fantasy platform operators (ESPN, Yahoo, NFL.com) all enforce minimum age requirements. A generalist engineer focused on functional features would not proactively include age-gating and COPPA compliance as a system requirement, yet its absence creates significant legal liability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall clearly disclose on all registration and league-entry pages whether the platform or any specific league involves an entry fee or prize, and shall not facilitate the collection or distribution of monetary prizes without verifying that such activity is lawful in the user's jurisdiction. The system shall block participation in paid leagues for users whose detected or self-declared jurisdiction prohibits paid fantasy sports contests (e.g., Arizona, Iowa, Louisiana, Montana, Washington state as of current US law), and shall surface a clear, user-facing explanation when access is restricted.
generated_requirements.rationale: Paid fantasy sports leagues occupy a complex legal landscape in the United States: while the Unlawful Internet Gambling Enforcement Act (UIGEA) of 2006 provides a federal carve-out for fantasy sports, several US states explicitly prohibit paid fantasy contests. Operating a paid league platform without jurisdiction-based access controls exposes the operator to state gambling law violations. This is a highly domain-specific compliance requirement that only a fantasy sports operations specialist would flag.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support the configuration and automated execution of an IR (Injured Reserve) or IL roster slot, allowing a team owner to place a player with an official 'IR', 'PUP', or 'NFI' NFL designation into a designated IR slot that does not count against the active roster size limit, subject to commissioner-configured rules on the number of IR slots and eligibility criteria. The system shall prevent a player in an IR slot from being inserted into the active starting lineup without first being moved off the IR slot.
generated_requirements.rationale: IR/IL roster slots are a standard feature in competitive fantasy football leagues, allowing owners to stash injured players without sacrificing active roster spots. The eligibility rules (which NFL designations qualify, how many IR slots exist, whether a player can be activated mid-season) are tightly coupled to official NFL injury designation data. Omitting this feature makes the platform non-competitive with industry-standard offerings and creates roster management inequities when key players suffer season-ending injuries.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall ingest official NFL bye-week schedules for all 32 NFL teams at the start of each season and shall surface a visual bye-week indicator on player cards, roster pages, and lineup selection interfaces for any player whose NFL team has a bye in the current or upcoming scoring week. The bye-week data shall be refreshed if the NFL publishes schedule corrections, and the system shall not award fantasy points to a player on bye.
generated_requirements.rationale: NFL bye weeks (each team has one per season, typically weeks 5–14) are a critical scheduling factor in fantasy football. Owners who inadvertently start a player on bye score zero points for that slot, which is a major source of user frustration and support tickets. Proactively surfacing bye-week information and ensuring the scoring engine correctly awards zero points during bye weeks is a domain-specific operational requirement that a generalist engineer would not independently identify.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a versioned, immutable record of the scoring ruleset in effect at the time each fantasy point calculation is performed, such that any historical matchup score can be audited and recalculated using the exact scoring parameters that were active during that scoring period, even if the commissioner subsequently modifies the league's scoring rules.
generated_requirements.rationale: Commissioners occasionally adjust scoring rules mid-season (e.g., adding a PPR component or changing touchdown point values). Without versioned scoring rule snapshots, the system cannot accurately audit or dispute historical scores, and retroactive rule changes could silently corrupt past matchup results. Fantasy league disputes over scoring discrepancies are common, and the ability to reproduce a historical score from its original ruleset is an essential operational and trust requirement that domain specialists recognize but generalists overlook.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow authorized finance staff and managers at branch, complex, district, and regional levels to create, edit, and submit annual budget entries appropriate to their organizational level.
generated_requirements.rationale: Core functional capability of the RFS — budget submission must be supported across all four tiers of PCG's organizational hierarchy.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall load revenue and expense data directly from the Business Data Warehouse (BDW) prior to each budgeting cycle, and shall refresh this data on a scheduled basis to ensure budget submissions reflect current BDW records.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly states that budget data is loaded from the BDW; a defined synchronization schedule is required to maintain data currency.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with the central User Administration System (UAS) to authenticate all users and retrieve their assigned roles and organizational level before granting access to any system functionality.
generated_requirements.rationale: Access control is delegated to the UAS; the system must consume UAS role and hierarchy data to enforce appropriate permissions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control such that each user can only view and modify budget data corresponding to their own organizational unit and, where applicable, subordinate units within the PCG hierarchy (branch < complex < district < regional).
generated_requirements.rationale: Prevents unauthorized cross-level data access; higher-level managers must be able to view roll-ups while lower-level staff are restricted to their own unit.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support an annual formula update process that allows a designated formula administrator to upload, validate, and activate new budget calculation formulae before the start of each budgeting cycle, without interrupting any budget submissions already in progress.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case states formulae are updated each year prior to the cycle; the update mechanism must be safe and non-disruptive to concurrent work.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically recalculate all dependent revenue and expense figures whenever an underlying formula is updated or when source BDW data is refreshed, and shall notify affected users of any changes to previously viewed figures.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ensures data integrity and user awareness; stale calculations could lead to incorrect budget submissions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an approval workflow whereby budget submissions flow upward through the organizational hierarchy (branch → complex → district → regional), requiring explicit approval or rejection at each level before progressing to the next.
generated_requirements.rationale: Multi-level hierarchical approval is implied by the organizational structure described; budget submissions must be formally reviewed at each tier.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect and handle BDW integration failures gracefully by queuing failed data synchronization requests, alerting the system administrator, and displaying a clear, user-friendly message to affected users without causing data loss or corruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: Failure handling at the BDW integration touchpoint is a key elicitation concern; the system must remain stable and informative when the BDW is unavailable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall detect and handle UAS integration failures gracefully by preventing new logins and displaying an appropriate service-unavailable message, while allowing already-authenticated sessions to continue in a read-only mode for a configurable grace period.
generated_requirements.rationale: UAS downtime must not abruptly terminate active user sessions or silently bypass access controls; a defined degraded-mode policy is required.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of at least 99.5% during the defined annual budgeting period, and at least 98% availability outside of that period, measured on a monthly basis.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case emphasizes high availability during the critical budgeting period; quantified SLA targets are needed to make this requirement testable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to any user-initiated page load or data retrieval action within 3 seconds under normal load conditions, and within 6 seconds under peak budgeting-period load, as measured at the server side.
generated_requirements.rationale: Responsiveness during the critical budgeting period is explicitly called out in the use case; concrete thresholds make this requirement verifiable.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 200 concurrent authenticated users during the peak budgeting period without degradation of response times below the thresholds defined in R011.
generated_requirements.rationale: Finance staff across all organizational levels will submit budgets simultaneously; the system must be sized for realistic concurrent load.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between the user's browser and the RFS server shall be encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher, and all sensitive budget data stored in the database shall be encrypted at rest using AES-256 or an equivalent approved algorithm.
generated_requirements.rationale: Financial data is sensitive; encryption in transit and at rest is a baseline security control aligned with ENET Securities' intranet standards.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a tamper-evident audit log recording all user authentication events, budget data modifications, formula updates, and approval actions, including the user identity, timestamp, and before/after values for any data change.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit trails are essential for financial systems to support compliance, fraud detection, and post-incident investigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system's user interface shall conform to ENET Securities' intranet standards for visual design, layout, typography, and color palette, and shall be fully functional on all browser versions designated as supported by those standards.
generated_requirements.rationale: The use case explicitly requires conformance to ENET Securities' intranet look, feel, and browser compatibility standards.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide contextual inline help and field-level validation messages written in plain language, enabling finance staff with no specialist IT knowledge to complete a budget submission without external assistance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Finance staff across multiple organizational levels will use the system; usability support reduces training overhead and submission errors.

generated_requirements.text: The system's codebase shall be structured using a documented layered architecture, with all business logic — including budget calculation formulae — isolated in a dedicated, independently deployable module to facilitate annual formula updates without requiring a full system redeployment.
generated_requirements.rationale: Annual formula changes are a recurring maintenance event; isolating formula logic reduces change risk and deployment complexity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall validate all new budget calculation formulae against a defined set of test cases and boundary conditions in a staging environment before they can be promoted to production, and shall prevent activation of any formula set that fails validation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Formula correctness is critical to budget integrity; a mandatory validation gate prevents erroneous formulae from affecting live submissions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on the existing ENET Securities intranet infrastructure without requiring the installation of client-side plugins or software beyond a standards-compliant web browser.
generated_requirements.rationale: As an intranet application, the system must operate within the constraints of the corporate environment and avoid client-side dependencies that require IT-managed installation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover automatically from a transient BDW or UAS connectivity failure within 5 minutes, re-establishing integration and resuming normal operation without requiring manual administrator intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transient integration failures are expected in any distributed system; automatic recovery minimizes operational impact and reduces dependency on manual intervention during the busy budgeting period.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a hard budget submission deadline per organizational level, automatically locking budget entries for a given unit once its submission window has closed, and shall prevent any further edits unless a designated approver explicitly grants a time-limited reopening with a recorded justification.
generated_requirements.rationale: Corporate budgeting cycles operate on strict calendar deadlines tied to board reporting, regulatory filings, and downstream planning processes. Without enforced submission windows and a controlled reopening mechanism, late or unauthorized edits can corrupt consolidated figures after roll-up has begun — a risk a generalist engineer would not anticipate. The audit trail on reopening justifications is also required for internal audit and SOX-style control evidence.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a prior-year actuals comparison view that displays, side-by-side for each revenue and expense line item, the current budget entry, the prior-year actual from BDW, and the calculated variance (both absolute and percentage), and shall flag any line item where the proposed budget deviates from the prior-year actual by more than a configurable threshold, requiring the submitting user to enter a written variance explanation before the submission can be progressed.
generated_requirements.rationale: Variance analysis against prior-year actuals is a fundamental control in corporate budgeting. Finance reviewers and auditors expect documented explanations for material deviations; without this, the approval workflow lacks the analytical substance needed for meaningful review. Unexplained outliers are a leading indicator of input error or manipulation, and this requirement directly supports management review controls expected under frameworks such as COSO and internal audit standards.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a top-down budget target distribution mechanism whereby regional or district-level managers can enter or import approved budget targets and have the system proportionally allocate those targets down to subordinate organizational units as a starting baseline, with subordinate units able to view but not delete the distributed target, and with the final rolled-up submission flagged if it materially exceeds the distributed target by more than a configurable tolerance.
generated_requirements.rationale: Most corporate budgeting processes combine bottom-up submissions with top-down targets set by senior management. Without a target distribution feature, the system only supports bottom-up entry, which means there is no mechanism to communicate board-approved spending envelopes to lower levels, and no automated check that consolidated submissions stay within sanctioned limits. This is a core budgeting domain concept that a generalist engineer would not include by default.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall produce a consolidated budget roll-up report at each organizational level (complex, district, regional) that aggregates all approved subordinate submissions into a single structured view, exportable in at least Microsoft Excel (.xlsx) and PDF formats, with each line item traceable to its contributing organizational unit.
generated_requirements.rationale: Consolidated roll-up reporting is a non-negotiable deliverable in any multi-level corporate budgeting process. Finance leadership, CFO offices, and external auditors require consolidated views that reconcile to the sum of parts. Excel export is specifically required because downstream financial models, board packs, and regulatory submissions are almost universally built in Excel in corporate finance environments. Traceability to contributing units is essential for audit and management review.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain all submitted, approved, and rejected budget versions for a minimum of seven years, ensuring that any historical budget submission — including the specific formula set and BDW data snapshot active at the time of submission — can be retrieved and rendered in read-only form by authorized users for audit and regulatory review purposes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Financial records retention obligations under corporate governance frameworks (e.g., SOX Section 802, SEC rules, and many national equivalents) typically mandate a minimum seven-year retention period for financial planning records. Critically, it is not sufficient to retain only the final numbers — auditors require the ability to reconstruct the exact state of a submission, including the formulae and source data in effect at that time. A generalist engineer would likely implement only current-state storage without versioned snapshots.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall implement a budget version control mechanism that preserves each distinct saved state of a budget submission as a numbered version, allowing authorized users to view the full history of changes to a submission, compare any two versions side-by-side, and restore a prior version with a mandatory justification comment recorded in the audit log.
generated_requirements.rationale: Budget submissions in corporate environments undergo multiple iterative revisions before final approval. Without version control, there is no way to recover from erroneous edits, demonstrate the evolution of a submission to auditors, or resolve disputes between submitters and approvers about what figures were agreed. This is distinct from the audit log requirement (which records individual field changes) — version control provides a holistic snapshot at each save point, which is the unit of review in a budgeting workflow.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent the simultaneous editing of the same budget submission by more than one user, implementing an optimistic or pessimistic concurrency control mechanism that notifies a second user attempting to open a submission already being edited, and shall ensure that no data entered by one user is silently overwritten by another user's concurrent session.
generated_requirements.rationale: In a multi-level organization during a compressed budgeting window, it is common for a manager and a delegate (or two delegates) to attempt to edit the same budget unit simultaneously. Silent last-write-wins overwrites are a serious data integrity risk in financial systems and have caused material restatements in practice. This is a domain-specific edge case that a generalist engineer may address generically at the database level but would not typically surface as an explicit user-facing workflow control.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a configurable budget cycle calendar, maintained by a designated system administrator, that defines the open and close dates for each submission window at each organizational level, the formula activation date, the BDW data freeze date, and key approval deadlines, and shall display the current cycle status and remaining time to each upcoming deadline prominently on the authenticated user's home screen.
generated_requirements.rationale: Corporate budgeting cycles are time-critical and involve multiple interdependent milestones across different organizational levels. Without a centrally managed, visible cycle calendar, users miss deadlines, administrators cannot enforce submission windows programmatically, and the system cannot automate deadline-driven state transitions (e.g., auto-locking submissions). Prominent deadline visibility directly reduces the volume of help-desk contacts and late-submission exceptions during the budgeting period.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate automated email or intranet notification alerts to relevant users and their designated alternates at configurable intervals before key budget cycle deadlines (e.g., 5 days, 2 days, and 1 day prior), and shall send immediate alerts upon budget rejection at any approval level, including the rejection reason entered by the approver, so that submitters can take corrective action within the remaining submission window.
generated_requirements.rationale: Deadline-driven notification is a standard operational requirement in enterprise budgeting systems and directly reduces the risk of missed submissions, which can delay board reporting and regulatory filings. Rejection notifications with reasons are particularly critical: without them, submitters are unaware their budget has been sent back, and the compressed budgeting window may expire before they discover the rejection through manual checking. This is a workflow completeness gap a generalist engineer would commonly overlook.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce segregation of duties such that the user who submits a budget entry at any organizational level cannot also be the approving user for that same entry at the next level in the hierarchy, and shall prevent any single user account from holding both submitter and approver roles for the same organizational unit within the same budget cycle.
generated_requirements.rationale: Segregation of duties (SoD) over budget submission and approval is a fundamental internal control requirement under SOX, COSO, and most corporate governance frameworks. Allowing the same individual to both prepare and approve their own budget submission eliminates a key detective control against manipulation or error. External auditors specifically test for SoD violations in financial systems, and the absence of this control would likely result in an audit finding or a compensating control requirement. A generalist engineer would implement role-based access but would not necessarily enforce cross-role SoD constraints at the workflow level.

sme_subject: corporate financial planning and budgeting analyst with enterprise intranet application experience

ground_truth_requirements.text: The product interface should be fast. The response time of the product interface should not exceed 2 seconds 90% of the time. For 10% of the time the response time should not be more than 10 seconds.not be more than 10 seconds.
